## User

我们的 SAVER 是否具有在有限观测预算下，模型必须决定该看哪里、当前已获取的证据是否已经足够，以及何时发出预警的设计呢。

## Assistant

我直接按三个子问题来回答，并对应到当前代码里的真实实现，不空谈。

## Assistant

有，但要实事求是地说：`当前 SAVER 已经具备这三个核心设计的雏形，而且代码里是落地了的；不过它还不是最严格、最论文化的“统一预算决策系统”，而是一个“有限轮次 + 有限帧数 + verifier/alert 决策”的版本。`

**1. 在有限观测预算下，模型必须决定该看哪里**
这点是有的。

- 初始只能看有限 `preview`，默认 `8` 帧，见 [config.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/config.py#L32) 和 [dataset.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/dataset.py#L292)。
- 每次工具调用最多只返回有限帧，`scan_timeline/seek_evidence` 都受 `MAX_NUM_KEY_FRAMES=8` 限制，见 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L14) 和 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L42)。
- agent 不能无限搜，rollout 有 `max_turns`，见 [rollout.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/rollout.py#L46)。

所以当前设计确实要求模型在有限观察里决定：
- 是先全局扫一遍还是直接定点搜
- 搜哪个时间段
- 用什么 query
- 还要不要继续搜

**2. 模型必须判断当前证据是否已经足够**
这点也是有的，而且这恰好是 SAVER 和普通 search agent 的关键区别。

- `verify_hypothesis` 明确就是在问“当前证据子集是否足以支撑 claim / alert / finalize”，见 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L196)。
- `finalize_case` 是最终收束动作，只有当证据足够时才该做，见 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L218)。
- 我们后面的 verifier 设计也已经明确不是“答案对不对”，而是“当前证据集是否足够、拿掉会不会塌、只保留这些是否还能站住”。

所以从任务定义上讲，SAVER 已经不是“看完就答”，而是“搜证据 -> 评估证据是否足够 -> 再决定 finalize”。

**3. 模型必须决定何时发出预警**
这点也有。

- `emit_alert` 是独立动作，不等于 `finalize_case`，见 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L165)。
- alert 有时机语义，比如 `earliest_alert_sec`，数据和 reward/verifier 里也有 `justified / premature / late` 这些状态。

这意味着当前 SAVER 的任务已经不是单纯 anomaly classification，而是：
- 何时开始相信异常存在
- 证据够不够支撑提前 alert
- 该 alert 还是再等等
- 最后何时 finalize

**但要加一句关键限定**
当前实现虽然具备这三层设计，`但它还不是最严格的“统一观测预算”版本`。

现在更准确地说，它是：

- `有限 preview`
- `有限单次工具帧数`
- `有限交互轮数`
- `证据充分性验证`
- `alert 时机决策`

而不是 TimeSearch-R 那种特别明确的统一 `video token budget / max_frames / total_pixels` 风格。

所以结论是：

`是的，我们的 SAVER 已经具有“有限观测下决定看哪里、判断证据是否足够、决定何时预警”的核心任务设计。`
但如果你问“它是否已经以最严谨、最强形式实现成一个统一预算约束的 agent system”，那答案是：`还差最后一层统一化。`

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接把这句话再推进成一个更尖锐的判断：

`我们当前 SAVER 到底是不是一个真正的 budgeted active anomaly agent？`
我可以按“任务定义、当前代码、论文表述”三层给你下一个非常明确的结论。

## User

他们 SFT 最值得借鉴的，不是 loss 本身，而是 tool schema + 多轮 assistant supervision + 明确的视频预算。TimeSearch-R 用真实 function schema 生成 tool-use prompt，而不是手写说明，见 GitHub 源码里的 time_r1/prompt/tool_use.py。这点我建议直接借。先完成这个借鉴，意思是我们要重新处理Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl这个文件吗

## Assistant

我先核一下我们现有的 `tool_registry` 和数据层分工，这样我能准确告诉你“要不要重做 canonical 标注文件”，以及具体该重生成哪一层产物。

## Assistant

不是，`先借鉴 TimeSearch-R 的 tool schema prompt` 这一步，`不需要重做` [msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl)。

原因很直接：

- [msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl) 是 `canonical annotation`，里面没有渲染后的 `messages`
- [msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl) 和 [msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl) 也没有 `messages`，它们主要是结构化任务字段和 `tool_io`
- 真正把 `system prompt / tool prompt` 烘焙进去的是 [msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl)，这个文件里已经有 `messages`

所以这一步正确的处理方式是：

1. 改代码  
改 [tool_registry.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tool_registry.py) 和 [prompts.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/prompts.py)，把现在的手写 tool contract 换成 schema-driven prompt。

2. 重生成 prepared SFT  
重跑 `train_saver_sft.py --prepare-only`，生成新的 `prepared_sft.jsonl`。

3. 重新训练 SFT  
因为旧的 SFT checkpoint 是在旧 prompt 分布上训出来的，最好不要继续混用。

4. 后续 rollout / RL 重新跑  
因为新 SFT checkpoint 会改变策略起点，旧 rollout 和旧 RL 结果也不该和新 prompt 混在一起比较。

你现在`不需要`重做的文件：

- [msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl)
- [msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl)
- [msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl)

你现在`需要`重做的主要是：

- [msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl)
- 基于它训练出来的 SFT checkpoint
- 之后的新 rollout / score / RL 实验产物

一句话总结：

`这一步是改“消息构造层”和“训练样本层”，不是改原始标注层。`

```bash
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

python train_saver_sft.py \
  --data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl \
  --data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --include-splits train \
  --prepare-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.schema_prompt.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --prepare-only \
  --validate-prepared-data
```

```bash
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.schema_prompt.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --include-splits train \
  --model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_schema_prompt_qwen3vl_8b \
  --eval-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl \
  --eval-data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --eval-include-splits test \
  --eval-rollout-max-turns 6 \
  --eval-verifier-backend heuristic \
  --lora \
  --bf16 \
  --gradient-checkpointing \
  --per-device-train-batch-size 1 \
  --gradient-accumulation-steps 16 \
  --learning-rate 1e-5 \
  --num-train-epochs 2 \
  --logging-steps 5 \
  --attn-implementation flash_attention_3 \
  --max-image-side 640 \
  --max-total-images 44
```

如果你想走“和 TimeSearch-R 更像”的那条精确路径，可以额外在你的环境里装 `qwen_agent`；不装也能跑，只是会走我写的本地 schema fallback。下一步我建议直接继续做两件事之一：

- 把 `prepared_sft` 里的 `<think>` 从占位符升级成更真实的 reasoning trace
- 把 SFT 的视频预算也统一成更像 TimeSearch-R 的显式配置层

**1. 先做一个很快的 sanity check**

conda activate qwen3-vl

**2. 重生成 prepared_sft**
这一步很重要，因为 `system prompt` 会被烘焙进 prepared 数据。

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

python train_saver_sft.py \
  --data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl \
  --data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --include-splits train \
  --prepare-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.schema_prompt.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --prepare-only \
  --validate-prepared-data
```

**3. 用新的 prepared_sft 重训 SFT**
```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.schema_prompt.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --include-splits train \
  --model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_schema_prompt_qwen3vl_8b \
  --eval-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl \
  --eval-data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --eval-include-splits test \
  --eval-rollout-max-turns 6 \
  --eval-verifier-backend heuristic \
  --lora \
  --bf16 \
  --gradient-checkpointing \
  --per-device-train-batch-size 1 \
  --gradient-accumulation-steps 16 \
  --learning-rate 1e-5 \
  --num-train-epochs 2 \
  --logging-steps 5 \
  --attn-implementation flash_attention_3 \
  --max-image-side 640 \
  --max-total-images 44
```

**4. SFT 训完后再做后续**
新的 checkpoint 出来后，再：
- 重新跑 batch rollout
- 重新 score / summarize
- 再用这个新 SFT checkpoint 做 RL 起点

一句话说，你现在最该做的是：

`验证 qwen_agent prompt 已生效 -> 重生成 prepared_sft -> 重训 SFT`

## User

当前 SFT 几乎没有在教模型“真实推理”，只是在教固定占位符式 <think>。在 training_data.py (line 20) 到 training_data.py (line 40)，每种工具对应的 <think> 都是固定短语，例如 inspect_global_context、verify_current_evidence。但系统提示又要求“Think carefully before each action”，见 prompts.py (line 24)。这会造成明显的 train-test mismatch：模型被要求多轮 reasoning，但 warm-start 实际没学到 reasoning，只学到标签壳子。对一个依赖“何时 search / 何时 verify / 何时 finalize”的 agent，这个问题是真正会伤性能的。
请你再来解决这个问题

代码层面这个风险是成立的：当前训练只做了图像裁剪，没有做显式文本/token 截断，长轨迹样本确实可能把 prompt/history/tool JSON 越堆越长。下一步我会看两件事：

## Assistant

我这边的判断先说在前面：TimeSearch-R 那种“显式 video token budget”只能解决一半问题。它能稳定视觉 tokens，但不能单独解决我们这里的长历史 JSON / 多轮 tool observation 文本膨胀。对 SAVER 更稳的做法应该是“双预算”：
- 视觉预算：限制 preview/tool 图像与视频 tokens
- 文本预算：显式 `max_seq_length`，并在 tokenizer 前做历史消息裁剪，避免把旧轮次工具输出无限累积进来

我准备直接把这套预算接进训练代码和 README。先从测试下手，把“超长消息会被显式裁剪且标签仍然对齐”这个行为卡住。

## Assistant

我现在会把这次修法收敛成一个尽量稳的方案，不走“大改整个数据结构”的路线，而是借 TimeSearch-R 的思路做两层显式预算：
- `max_seq_length`: 传到 tokenizer，保证底层不会无限长
- 历史消息预算: 在 tokenizer 前保留最近若干条 tool/assistant 历史，避免老 observation 抢占文本长度

这样既能控稳定性，也不会把现有 prepared SFT 全推翻。

根因已经定位并修好了。

这次报错不是数据坏了，而是我们之前的实现对 `Qwen3-VL` 不兼容：
- 含图样本还保留所有图片
- 同时把 `max_length/truncation` 直接传给 processor
- `Qwen3-VL` 会先把每张图展开成多模态占位 token，再检查文本里的 `<image>` token 数是否一致
- 一旦截断把一部分 `<image>` token 截掉，但图片列表还在，就会直接报你看到的这个错

我已经把它改成更像 TimeSearch-R 思路的“token-aware budget”版本了，关键改动在：
- [_tokenize_chat]( /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L222 )
  - 含多模态输入时，不再对 Qwen processor 直接做 `truncation=max_length`
- [_fit_messages_to_budget]( /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L331 )
  - 先按真实 token 长度试编码
  - 超预算就先裁最旧的多模态内容，再裁最旧的历史消息
  - 直到样本真正落进 `max_seq_length`
- [SingleExampleMultimodalCollator]( /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L551 )
  - 现在走这条新的 budget-aware fitting 路径
- 我还补了回归测试：
  - [test_saver_agent_training.py]( /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_training.py#L453 )

我本地已经验证过：
- `python -m unittest tests.test_saver_agent_training tests.test_train_saver_sft tests.test_train_saver_rl tests.test_full_pipeline_script`
  - `Ran 37 tests ... OK`
- `python -m unittest discover -s tests`
  - `Ran 126 tests ... OK`

你现在不需要重做 `prepared SFT`，直接重跑原来的 `train_saver_sft.py` 命令就可以。

顺便回答你前面那个判断：是的，TimeSearch-R 那种显式 `video token budget` 思路是对的。这个 bug 本身就说明“按图片张数裁剪”不够，必须按真实多模态 token 去控预算。现在这版已经朝那个方向修正了，只是目前是“用全局 `max_seq_length` 做 token-aware 自适应裁剪”，还不是单独暴露一个 `max_vision_tokens` 参数。

如果你这次重新跑之后不再报错，但感觉吞吐还是偏慢，那就不是这个 bug 了，而是预算偏大。我建议你那时优先调小：
- `--max-total-images`
- `--max-image-side`

当前这版先足够把 SFT 跑通。

## User

我直接 bash full_pipeline.sh 就行了吗

## Assistant

可以，但不是 `bash full_pipeline.sh`，而是运行这个脚本：[00_full_pipeline.sh](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh)

最直接的跑法是：

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code
bash scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh
```

如果你用默认目录，这个脚本现在就能跑通，而且已经默认打开了 CEA-GRPO 和新的双预算控制。

但有 3 个很重要的前提你要注意：

- 如果你只是因为刚才那个 `Qwen3-VL` token mismatch 报错而中断，现在可以直接重新跑这个脚本，不需要重做 prepared SFT。
- 如果你的 `prepared_sft` 是在最近这批 `oracle_verifier_feedback / branch supervision` 修复之前生成的，`full_pipeline` 会因为“文件已存在”而跳过 Stage 1，所以会继续用旧 prepared 数据。这种情况下，先删掉旧文件再跑：
```bash
rm -f /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl
rm -f /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl
```
- 如果你已经有完整的 `SFT_OUTPUT_DIR` checkpoint，脚本会自动跳过 SFT。也就是说，哪怕你重建了 prepared SFT，只要旧 SFT checkpoint 还在，它还是不会重训 SFT。要强制重训，就再删掉或改名这个目录：
```bash
mv /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_eval_ddp \
   /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_eval_ddp.bak
```

如果你想要“用最新代码完整重跑一遍 SFT -> rollout/score/summarize -> RL”，我建议你用这组最稳的命令：

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

rm -f /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl
rm -f /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl
mv /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_eval_ddp \
   /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_eval_ddp.bak 2>/dev/null || true

bash scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh
```

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你一版“保守重跑”和“一键全量重跑”两套命令，按你现在目录一字不改就能执行。

## User

我应该如何重新准备 sft 数据呢，请你教我一下

## Assistant

可以，我给你最稳、最实用的两种方式。

如果你的目标只是“用最新代码重新生成 SFT 训练样本”，通常只需要重做 `prepared_sft.jsonl`；不一定要重做全部标注文件。

**一、最推荐：只重生成 prepared SFT**

这适合你已经有最新的 [msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl)，只是想让 SFT 样本吃到最新的：
- schema-driven prompt
- `oracle_verifier_feedback`
- `verify -> revise/continue/finalize` 分支监督

直接运行：

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

rm -f /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl

python train_saver_sft.py \
  --data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl \
  --data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --include-splits train \
  --prepare-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --prepare-only \
  --validate-prepared-data \
  --validate-materialization \
  --progress-every 25
```

这一步做完后，你的 SFT 数据就是新的了。

然后正式训练 SFT 时，用：

```bash
torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --include-splits train \
  --model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_eval_ddp \
  --lora \
  --bf16 \
  --gradient-checkpointing \
  --max-seq-length 4096 \
  --keep-recent-text-messages 12 \
  --max-image-side 640 \
  --max-total-images 44 \
  --per-device-train-batch-size 1 \
  --gradient-accumulation-steps 1 \
  --learning-rate 1e-5 \
  --num-train-epochs 1.0 \
  --logging-steps 5 \
  --attn-implementation flash_attention_3
```

**二、最彻底：从 canonical 开始整套重建**

如果你担心上游 `agent_train` 也不是最新逻辑，就从 [msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl) 全量重建。

先重建 `agent_train`：

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

python convert_to_saver_agent.py \
  --input /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl \
  --output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl \
  --adapter msad_saver_qwen \
  --mode agent_train \
  --include-splits train
```

如果你还想单独留一份 `oracle_sft.jsonl` 方便检查，也可以再跑：

```bash
python convert_to_saver_agent.py \
  --input /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_with_qwen.jsonl \
  --output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl \
  --adapter msad_saver_qwen \
  --mode oracle_sft \
  --include-splits train
```

然后再跑刚才那条 `--prepare-only` 命令生成 `prepared_sft.jsonl`。

**你现在最该选哪种？**

按我们当前进度，我建议你直接用“方式一”就够了。  
因为现在 `train_saver_sft.py --prepare-only` 会基于 `agent_train` 动态重建最新 oracle 轨迹，所以通常没必要每次都从 canonical 全量重转。

**一个检查点**

prepare 成功后，你可以先看一下文件行数确认不是空文件：

```bash
wc -l /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl
```

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你一份“只重准备 SFT 数据，不动 RL”的完整命令清单，按顺序复制执行就行。

我刚把 `full_pipeline` 的 SFT 文本预算入口也补上了，所以现在这套参数是可以直接执行的，不会再出现“README 推荐，但脚本没接”的情况。接下来我给你一套我认为最适合你这台 4xH200 的正式版配置。

## Assistant

可以。我建议你把 `full_pipeline` 跑成“8B policy + 32B verifier + 更大上下文预算”的正式版，而不是直接把 policy 也切到 32B。

原因很简单：当前 RL 里同时要持有 `policy / old policy / reference policy`，32B policy 虽然你也未必放不下，但训练和 rollout 会明显更慢；把额外显存优先用在更大的文本/视觉预算，以及更强的 32B verifier，通常更划算。顺手说一句，我刚把 [00_full_pipeline.sh](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh#L101) 也补好了，现在它已经支持 `SFT_MAX_SEQ_LENGTH` 和 `SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TEXT_MESSAGES`，不会再出现 SFT 文本预算喂不进去的问题；对应回归测试也补了，在 [test_full_pipeline_script.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_full_pipeline_script.py#L197)。

我给你的推荐参数是这套“正式主跑”配置：

- `MODEL_PATH` 继续用 `qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct`
- `ROLLOUT_VERIFIER_MODEL_PATH` 和 `RL_VERIFIER_MODEL_PATH` 用 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`
- `SFT_MAX_SEQ_LENGTH=6144`
- `SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TEXT_MESSAGES=16`
- `SFT_MAX_IMAGE_SIDE=768`
- `SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_IMAGE_MESSAGES=4`
- `SFT_MAX_TOTAL_IMAGES=56`
- `SFT_NUM_TRAIN_EPOCHS=2.0`
- `SFT_GRADIENT_ACCUMULATION_STEPS=16`
- `SFT_EVAL_MAX_RECORDS=160`
- `ROLLOUT_COUNT=0`
  这表示 Stage 3 跑完整个 `test` split
- `ROLLOUT_DO_SAMPLE=0`
  主评估建议 deterministic
- `ROLLOUT_VERIFIER_BACKEND=hybrid`
- `ROLLOUT_VERIFIER_HYBRID_ALPHA=0.8`
- `RL_NUM_ITERATIONS=4`
- `RL_ROLLOUT_COUNT=32`
- `RL_NUM_GENERATIONS=6`
- `RL_NUM_TRAIN_EPOCHS=1.0`
  这里不要因为显存大就加太高，PPO/GRPO 对 stale rollout 过多 epoch 容易过拟合
- `RL_GRADIENT_ACCUMULATION_STEPS=8`
- `RL_MAX_SEQ_LENGTH=6144`
- `RL_KEEP_RECENT_TEXT_MESSAGES=16`
- `RL_MAX_IMAGE_SIDE=768`
- `RL_KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_IMAGE_MESSAGES=4`
- `RL_MAX_TOTAL_IMAGES=56`
- `RL_VERIFIER_BACKEND=hybrid`
- `RL_VERIFIER_HYBRID_ALPHA=0.8`
- `RL_KL_BETA=0.02`
- `RL_MIN_WEIGHT=0.1`
- `RL_ADVANTAGE_CLIP=3.0`
- `RL_EVAL_MAX_RECORDS=160`

直接可跑的命令我给你整理好了。这个版本会用新的输出目录，避免因为旧 checkpoint 存在而被脚本自动跳过：

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

MODEL_ROOT=/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs \
MODEL_PATH=/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct \
ROLLOUT_VERIFIER_MODEL_PATH=/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct \
RL_VERIFIER_MODEL_PATH=/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct \
SFT_OUTPUT_DIR=/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_h200_ctx6k \
RL_OUTPUT_DIR=/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_cea_grpo_h200_ctx6k \
ROLLOUT_RUN_NAME=sft_rollout_eval_h200_ctx6k \
VALIDATE_MATERIALIZATION=1 \
VALIDATION_MAX_EXAMPLES=256 \
SFT_NPROC_PER_NODE=4 \
SFT_NUM_TRAIN_EPOCHS=2.0 \
SFT_GRADIENT_ACCUMULATION_STEPS=16 \
SFT_MAX_IMAGE_SIDE=768 \
SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_IMAGE_MESSAGES=4 \
SFT_MAX_TOTAL_IMAGES=56 \
SFT_MAX_SEQ_LENGTH=6144 \
SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TEXT_MESSAGES=16 \
SFT_EVAL_MAX_RECORDS=160 \
ROLLOUT_COUNT=0 \
ROLLOUT_DO_SAMPLE=0 \
ROLLOUT_VERIFIER_BACKEND=hybrid \
ROLLOUT_VERIFIER_HYBRID_ALPHA=0.8 \
RL_NPROC_PER_NODE=4 \
RL_NUM_ITERATIONS=4 \
RL_ROLLOUT_COUNT=32 \
RL_NUM_GENERATIONS=6 \
RL_POLICY_DO_SAMPLE=1 \
RL_POLICY_TEMPERATURE=0.7 \
RL_POLICY_TOP_P=0.9 \
RL_KL_BETA=0.02 \
RL_MIN_WEIGHT=0.1 \
RL_ADVANTAGE_CLIP=3.0 \
RL_EVAL_MAX_RECORDS=160 \
RL_VERIFIER_BACKEND=hybrid \
RL_VERIFIER_HYBRID_ALPHA=0.8 \
RL_NUM_TRAIN_EPOCHS=1.0 \
RL_GRADIENT_ACCUMULATION_STEPS=8 \
RL_MAX_IMAGE_SIDE=768 \
RL_KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_IMAGE_MESSAGES=4 \
RL_MAX_TOTAL_IMAGES=56 \
RL_MAX_SEQ_LENGTH=6144 \
RL_KEEP_RECENT_TEXT_MESSAGES=16 \
bash scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh
```

如果你觉得 32B verifier 太慢，只改这两个变量就行，别动主训练配置：

```bash

RL_VERIFIER_MODEL_PATH=/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct
```

## User

[rank 0/4] starting Trainer.train()
Epoch 1/2:   0%|                                                                                              | 0/40 [00:00<?, ?it/s到这一步就卡住不动了是为什么呢‘

## Assistant

我先按“为什么看起来卡在 `0/40`”这个现象拆一下。最常见不是训练真死了，而是首个 batch 的数据物化、视频解码或多卡同步在跑，但我们现在日志不够细。先检查训练入口、collator 和数据读取这几段，看看是不是首 batch 前的 CPU/I/O 开销过大。

## Assistant

我再多确认一层：训练阶段是不是在反复从原始视频解码预览帧，而不是命中 `.frame_cache`。如果是这样，`0/40` 不动在你的机器上通常就是“首批样本还在从共享存储拉视频并解码”，不是 optimizer 真卡死。

## Assistant

我先按调试流程把结论收束一下，再给你最有用的排查顺序和参数建议，避免你反复盯着那个 `0/40`。

## Assistant

我再补两处精确定位给你：一处是为什么进度条会在 `0/40` 停很久，另一处是首个 batch 前到底在做什么数据活。这样你能判断它是“慢”还是“真挂了”。

## Assistant

我再看一下仓库里有没有现成的 `frame_cache` 生成链路。如果没有，这个“看起来卡住”的核心优化其实就是补一个离线 frame cache 预处理脚本。

## Assistant

大概率不是“真卡死”，而是首个 `optimizer step` 还没完成，所以进度条一直显示 `0/40`。

原因有两层，而且你这次两层都叠上了：

1. 这个进度条统计的是 `global_step`，不是 micro-batch。
在 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L1462) 到 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L1495)，进度条只有在 `on_step_end` 才更新；而 `step` 是按 `gradient_accumulation_steps` 聚合后的 optimizer step。
如果你现在是 `gradient_accumulation_steps=16`，那要等每个 rank 先跑完 16 个前向/反向，主进度条才会从 `0/40` 变成 `1/40`。

2. 你的每个 micro-batch 现在都很重。
在 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L595) 到 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L598)，`__getitem__` 会把 `image_ref` 重新物化成真实帧。
如果旁边没有 `.frame_cache`，它会退回到原始视频读取，在 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L423) 到 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L441) 先尝试 cache，不命中就走 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L483) 到 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L505) 的 `decord/cv2` 读原视频。
如果视频在共享存储上，4 个 rank 同时这样干，首个 step 很容易看起来像“挂住”。

所以，`0/40` 往往表示：
“还在准备并跑首个 optimizer step”，
不是“训练循环没动”。

你现在最该做的是开两个监控窗口看它到底是“慢”还是“真挂”：

```bash
watch -n 1 "nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,utilization.gpu,utilization.memory,memory.used,memory.total --format=csv,noheader"
```

```bash
pidstat -dru -p $(pgrep -d, -f train_saver_sft.py) 1
```

判断标准很简单：

- 如果 GPU 利用率会波动，或者 CPU / 磁盘读很高，那它只是首 step 很慢，继续等。
- 如果 10 分钟以上 GPU 基本全是 0，CPU / 磁盘也几乎不动，那就更像是某个 rank 卡在视频读取或解码上了。

我更推荐你的正式训练把 SFT 调成更适合 H200 的设置，不要再用太大的累积步数。你显存够，完全没必要让首步这么久。下一次跑建议先改成：

```bash
SFT_GRADIENT_ACCUMULATION_STEPS=4
SFT_MAX_SEQ_LENGTH=4096
SFT_MAX_IMAGE_SIDE=640
SFT_MAX_TOTAL_IMAGES=44
SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_IMAGE_MESSAGES=2
SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TEXT_MESSAGES=12
```

也就是：

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

SFT_GRADIENT_ACCUMULATION_STEPS=4 \
SFT_MAX_SEQ_LENGTH=4096 \
SFT_MAX_IMAGE_SIDE=640 \
SFT_MAX_TOTAL_IMAGES=44 \
SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_IMAGE_MESSAGES=2 \
SFT_KEEP_RECENT_TEXT_MESSAGES=12 \
bash scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh
```

如果你愿意，我下一步建议直接给你补一个 `build_frame_cache.py`。这会比反复调参数更本质，因为它能把训练时最慢的“从原始视频回读帧”这件事提前离线做掉。

你现在最实用的用法有两条。

先给 SFT 训练集建 cache：

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code

python build_frame_cache.py \
  --data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl \
  --data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --include-splits train \
  --cache-video-fps 2.0 \
  --max-cache-frames 256 \
  --progress-every 50 \
  --summary-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/frame_cache_train_summary.json
```

再给 full pipeline 里会用到的评测视频建 cache：

```bash
python build_frame_cache.py \
  --data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl \
  --data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --include-splits test \
  --cache-video-fps 2.0 \
  --max-cache-frames 256 \
  --progress-every 50 \
  --summary-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/frame_cache_test_summary.json
```

如果你怀疑旧 cache 不对，重建时加 `--overwrite` 就行。

做完这两步，再跑：

```bash
bash scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh
```

会比现在顺很多，因为训练时 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L595) 的 `image_ref` 物化会优先命中 `.frame_cache`，不再大量回源解码原视频。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续把它接进 `00_full_pipeline.sh`，加一个可选的 `PREBUILD_FRAME_CACHE=1` 开关，让你以后不需要手动先跑两次。

已经接好了。现在 `full_pipeline` 虽然没有单独新增一个 bash 级 `check_cache.sh` 阶段，但它调用到的关键 Python 入口都会主动打印 cache 状态，而且一旦真实回退到原视频解码，终端会明确出现 `[cache-warning]`。

## User

训练中还有什么数据可以离线处理吗

## Assistant

我先结合当前代码看一眼哪些环节还在运行时做重活，给你一个“值得离线化 / 不值得离线化”的清单。

## Assistant

我再补一个点：看看 `feature_cache / embedding` 现在到底有没有真正被后续流程消费，避免给你推荐一个“其实现在没用上”的离线项。

## Assistant

有，而且按“收益 / 实现成本”来看，我建议分成三档。

**最值得离线化**
- `prepared_sft.jsonl`
  - 这个你已经在做了，收益很高。
  - 它把 schema-driven prompt、多轮轨迹、`oracle_verifier_feedback` 都提前展开了，SFT 时不必再重复构造。
- `.frame_cache`
  - 这个你现在也已经有了，是目前最重要的离线项。
  - 训练和 rollout 最重的 I/O 基本就是视频读帧。
- `rollouts.scored.jsonl` 和 `summary.json`
  - 这个也已经在做。
  - 一旦 rollout 固定，reward / verifier / summarize 都应尽量复用，不要反复现算。

**我建议下一步新增的离线化**
- `RL training examples`
  - 现在 `train_saver_rl.py` 里，打完分之后还会在线把 `scored_rollouts -> reward/CEA-GRPO examples` 再构一遍。
  - 这一步虽然不如视频解码重，但如果你经常“同一批 rollout 反复调 RL 超参”，它会重复做很多工作。
  - 最适合新增一个类似：
    - `prepare_saver_rl_examples.py`
    - 输入 `rollouts.scored.jsonl`
    - 输出 `training_examples.jsonl`
    - 然后 `train_saver_rl.py --prepared-training-examples ...`
  - 这是我认为你现在最值得补的下一个离线阶段。

**可以做，但优先级没那么高**
- `cache manifest / cache coverage report`
  - 比如提前生成每个 split 的 `video_path -> has_frame_cache`
  - 主要用于检查，不是性能大头。
- `prepared eval subset`
  - 如果你每次只评固定一批 test 样本，可以把索引清单固定下来，减少反复切分和扫描。
- `offline reference index`
  - `score_saver_rollout.py` 每次会重新读 reference JSONL。
  - 可以做轻量索引缓存，但收益通常不大。

**当前不建议现在做**
- `.feature_cache`
  - 代码里虽然会加载 `.feature_cache`，但当前主流程里基本没有真正消费这个 `embedding`。
  - 也就是说现在就算你花时间预计算 feature，收益很有限。
- 把 `prepared_sft` 里的 `image_ref` 全部改成 inline image
  - 会让文件非常大，I/O 和存储都变差，不划算。
- 把 verifier 的 keep/drop/full 视图全离线化
  - 这类视图强依赖 rollout 时的 `claim`、`candidate_window_ids`、alert 决策。
  - 不是稳定静态数据，没法优雅地一次性预烘焙。

**一句话结论**
现在最值得继续离线化的，不是再去做别的视觉 cache，而是把 `scored rollouts -> RL training examples` 这一层单独固化下来。这样你后面调 `CEA-GRPO` 的很多实验都会轻松很多。

如果你认可，我下一步可以直接给你补一个 `prepare_saver_rl_examples.py`，把这层正式拆出来。

我顺手把脚本默认值也定位一下，尤其是 `SFT_GRADIENT_ACCUMULATION_STEPS` 和 `NPROC_PER_NODE`，因为这会影响你对“卡住”的直觉。

## Assistant

## Assistant

是的，这个现象基本说明你当前的 SFT 不是“GPU 算不动”，而是“GPU 经常在等 CPU / I/O 喂数据”。  
如果 `volatile gpu util` 大部分时间是 `0`，而 `pwr` 也基本不抬升，最常见含义就是：显卡已经把模型放进去了，但前向/反向并没有持续发生。

**结论**
当前这套 SFT 里，最影响速度的不是模型本身，而是“单样本、多图、多轮消息”的主机侧准备链路。对你现在的代码，最可能的瓶颈顺序通常是：

- 视频帧读取是否命中 `.frame_cache`
- `image_ref -> image` 的同步 materialization
- `collator` 里的图像 resize、消息裁剪、反复 tokenization
- `per_device_train_batch_size=1` 带来的短促 GPU burst
- `gradient_accumulation_steps` 偏大导致一个 optimizer step 很久才出现
- epoch-end rollout eval 把训练节奏切断

**当前代码里真正会拖慢 SFT 的点**
- 数据如果没命中 `.frame_cache`，会直接 fallback 到原视频解码。入口在 [dataset.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/dataset.py#L197)；warning 在 [dataset.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/dataset.py#L344)。
- prepared SFT 训练时，`WeightedExampleDataset.__getitem__` 会同步把 `image_ref` 还原成图像，[training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L696)。如果 cache 没命中，会回退到 raw frame materialization，[training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L494)。
- collator 明确要求 `per_device_train_batch_size=1`，[training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L619)。这意味着每次 GPU 只吃一个样本，本来就很难把 H200 持续打满。
- collator 每个样本都会做一串 CPU 工作：消息裁剪、图片 resize、提取视觉输入、预算拟合，[training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L232) 和 [training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L645)。
- 最重的一点是 `_fit_messages_to_budget(...)` 会反复构建 chat text、反复 tokenization，必要时循环删旧图/旧历史再重试，[training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L416)。这非常容易把 GPU 饿住。
- `TrainingArguments` 在这里创建，[training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L1505)。你当前代码没有显式设置 dataloader worker；我刚在你的 `qwen3-vl` 环境里确认了 `transformers` 默认是 `dataloader_num_workers=0`。这意味着数据准备基本在主训练进程同步做。
- 训练脚本默认 `gradient_accumulation_steps` 不低。单独跑 SFT 脚本时默认是 `16`，[02_train_sft_with_rollout_eval.sh](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/scripts/02_train_sft_with_rollout_eval.sh#L24)；`full_pipeline` 里默认是 `4`，[00_full_pipeline.sh](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh#L87)。这会让“一个 step 很久才算完”的感觉更明显。
- 如果你不是用 `--prepared-data`，而是直接 `--data` 在 `torchrun` 下构样本，每个 rank 都会重复做数据准备，[train_saver_sft.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_sft.py#L365)。这会让训练开头尤其像“卡死”。
- 你还开了 epoch-end rollout eval 的话，每个 epoch 结束都会切去跑 rollout evaluation，[training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L1644)。这段时间不是纯 SFT 吞吐。

**为什么会看到 `volatile gpu util=0`**
- `volatile gpu util` 是瞬时采样值。你的 GPU 可能是“短时间算一下，然后长时间等数据”，于是大多数采样点都刚好落在等待区间。
- `batch size=1` 时，Qwen3-VL 的一次前后向可能是很短的 burst；如果前面 CPU 花很多时间准备这个样本，`nvidia-smi` 看上去就会像“几乎没算”。
- `pwr` 基本静态也支持这个判断：显存占着、模型驻留着，但算子没有持续跑起来。这是典型的 GPU starvation，不是 GPU 算力不够。

**你现在最该关注的几个日志**
- 训练开始时打印的 `training frame cache: cached=.../...`  
  这能直接看出 cache 覆盖率。
- 任何 `[cache-warning]`  
  只要看到它，就说明这个样本没有走理想缓存路径，而是在现场读原视频或原始帧。
- 如果你看到是从 `--data` 构建 prepared examples，而不是直接 `loading prepared SFT examples`，那训练前就已经有一大段 CPU 工作了。

**实话实说地判断**
对你现在这套代码，如果 GPU 大部分时间 `util=0`，最可能不是模型太轻，而是下面这个组合：
- `dataloader_num_workers=0`
- `per_device_train_batch_size=1`
- `image_ref` 同步 materialization
- 共享存储上的 `.frame_cache`/视频读取
- collator 里反复 tokenization 与 budget fitting

也就是说，这是“输入流水线太慢”，不是“算子太慢”。

**怎么快速验证是不是这个原因**
同时开这两个监控最有用：

```bash
watch -n 1 "nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,utilization.gpu,utilization.memory,memory.used,memory.total,power.draw --format=csv,noheader"
```

```bash
pidstat -dru -p $(pgrep -d, -f train_saver_sft.py) 1
```

如果你看到：
- GPU util 大部分时间低
- 但 CPU、磁盘读、进程上下文切换在动
- 终端还有 `[cache-warning]`

那就几乎可以确定是数据/CPU 侧瓶颈。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接把 SFT 再优化一轮，优先做这三件事：
1. 给 `run_weighted_sft` 接 `dataloader_num_workers / persistent_workers / prefetch_factor`
2. 进一步减少 collator 里的重复 tokenization
3. 把 `prepared_sft` 再往“更接近训练时直接可喂”的方向预烘焙一层

## User

给 run_weighted_sft 接 dataloader_num_workers / persistent_workers / prefetch_factor
进一步减少 collator 里的重复 tokenization
把 prepared_sft 再往“更接近训练时直接可喂”的方向预烘焙一层 这些操作会影响模型性能吗

## Assistant

会分情况。先把“性能”拆开说：

- 如果你说的是训练吞吐、GPU 利用率，这三项大概率都会明显改善。
- 如果你说的是最终任务指标、模型泛化，它们不应该天然变差，但前提是我们做的是“等价加速”，不是“偷偷改训练输入”。

我直接给你结论：

**1. `dataloader_num_workers / persistent_workers / prefetch_factor`**
- 对最终模型指标基本没有本质影响。
- 它主要影响的是“CPU 预处理能不能提前把 batch 准备好”。
- 风险不在精度，而在稳定性和 I/O：
  - worker 太多会把共享存储打爆
  - 多进程读视频/cache 可能让吞吐反而更差
  - 主机内存会涨
- 所以这是低风险优化，优先做。

**2. 减少 collator 里的重复 tokenization**
- 如果我们做的是“严格等价”的优化，基本不影响模型指标。
- 但如果为了省事，改成更激进裁剪、近似 token 计数、少保留一些上下文，那就会影响性能。
- 这项的关键边界是：
  - 最终 `input_ids / labels / token_advantages / 图像集合` 不变，就只是加速
  - 只要这些有变化，就可能影响结果

你现在最重的热点就在这里：
- [_fit_messages_to_budget](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L416)
- [SingleExampleMultimodalCollator](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L619)

所以这项可以做，但我会坚持“等价优化”，不做近似。

**3. 把 `prepared_sft` 再往前预烘焙一层**
- 这是三项里最容易碰到“会影响模型性能”的一项。
- 因为你一旦把更多东西提前固化，就会把当前训练配置一起固化进去，比如：
  - `max_seq_length`
  - `max_image_side`
  - `max_total_images`
  - `keep_recent_text_messages`
  - preview 帧策略
  - 甚至 Qwen processor 版本
- 如果后面你改了这些参数，但还继续用旧的 prepared 产物，训练输入就变成“静默错配”，这会真实伤性能。

所以这项不是不能做，而是要分两种：

- 安全预烘焙
  - 只烘焙 model-agnostic 内容
  - 比如 expanded trajectory、schema prompt、oracle verifier feedback、稳定的 `image_ref`
  - 这类基本不伤性能
- 高风险预烘焙
  - 直接烘焙 budget-trimmed messages、tokenized tensors、最终图像尺寸
  - 这类必须和模型/预算强绑定
  - 只适合同一模型、同一训练配置反复跑

**我的建议**
- 第 1 项可以直接做，低风险高收益。
- 第 2 项只做“等价缓存/等价复用”，不要改训练语义。
- 第 3 项要做，但做成“配置绑定缓存”，不能做成一个永远通用的 prepared 文件。

最稳的策略是两层产物：

- `prepared_sft.jsonl`
  - 仍然保持相对通用
- `prepared_sft.runtime_cache/`
  - 专门绑定某个配置
  - 名字里带上关键参数哈希

比如至少绑定这些：
- `model_path`
- `max_seq_length`
- `max_image_side`
- `max_image_pixels`
- `max_total_images`
- `keep_recent_tool_image_messages`
- `keep_recent_text_messages`
- `num_preview_frames`
- `preview_sampling_fps`

这样做的结果是：
- 吞吐会上去
- 最终指标理论上不变
- 也不会因为换了配置还误用旧缓存而静默掉点

一句话总结：
- 第 1 项基本不会伤模型性能。
- 第 2 项只要做成等价优化，也不会。
- 第 3 项会不会伤，取决于你是不是把“训练配置相关的裁剪结果”错误地当成通用数据复用。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以按这个“低风险版本”直接推进：
1. 先接 dataloader knobs
2. 再做 strict-equivalent collator caching
3. 最后设计一层带配置哈希的 `prepared_sft runtime cache`

先接 dataloader knobs

我先按你确认的顺序推进：先把 `run_weighted_sft` 的 dataloader 参数打通到脚本入口，再做不改变训练语义的 collator 等价缓存，并且先用测试把行为锁住。

测试设计我收敛成三条，够小也够稳：

- `train_saver_sft.main()` 真正把这些参数传给 `run_weighted_sft`

## Assistant

# Scientific Writing

## Overview

**This is the core skill for the deep research and writing tool**—combining AI-driven deep research with well-formatted written outputs. Every document produced is backed by comprehensive literature search and verified citations through the research-lookup skill.

Scientific writing is a process for communicating research with precision and clarity. Write manuscripts using IMRAD structure, citations (APA/AMA/Vancouver), figures/tables, and reporting guidelines (CONSORT/STROBE/PRISMA). Apply this skill for research papers and journal submissions.

**Critical Principle: Always write in full paragraphs with flowing prose. Never submit bullet points in the final manuscript.** Use a two-stage process: first create section outlines with key points using research-lookup, then convert those outlines into complete paragraphs.

## When to Use This Skill

This skill should be used when:
- Writing or revising any section of a scientific manuscript (abstract, introduction, methods, results, discussion)
- Structuring a research paper using IMRAD or other standard formats
- Formatting citations and references in specific styles (APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, IEEE)
- Creating, formatting, or improving figures, tables, and data visualizations
- Applying study-specific reporting guidelines (CONSORT for trials, STROBE for observational studies, PRISMA for reviews)
- Drafting abstracts that meet journal requirements (structured or unstructured)
- Preparing manuscripts for submission to specific journals
- Improving writing clarity, conciseness, and precision
- Ensuring proper use of field-specific terminology and nomenclature
- Addressing reviewer comments and revising manuscripts

## Visual Enhancement with Scientific Schematics

**⚠️ MANDATORY: Every scientific paper MUST include a graphical abstract plus 1-2 additional AI-generated figures using the scientific-schematics skill.**

This is not optional. Scientific papers without visual elements are incomplete. Before finalizing any document:
1. **ALWAYS generate a graphical abstract** as the first visual element
2. Generate at minimum ONE additional schematic or diagram using scientific-schematics
3. Prefer 3-4 total figures for comprehensive papers (graphical abstract + methods flowchart + results visualization + conceptual diagram)

### Graphical Abstract (REQUIRED)

**Every scientific writeup MUST include a graphical abstract.** This is a visual summary of your paper that:
- Appears before or immediately after the text abstract
- Captures the entire paper's key message in one image
- Is suitable for journal table of contents display
- Uses landscape orientation (typically 1200x600px)

**Generate the graphical abstract FIRST:**
```bash
python scripts/generate_schematic.py "Graphical abstract for [paper title]: [brief description showing workflow from input → methods → key findings → conclusions]" -o figures/graphical_abstract.png
```

**Graphical Abstract Requirements:**
- **Content**: Visual summary showing workflow, key methods, main findings, and conclusions
- **Style**: Clean, professional, suitable for journal TOC
- **Elements**: Include 3-5 key steps/concepts with connecting arrows or flow
- **Text**: Minimal labels, large readable fonts
- Log: `[HH:MM:SS] GENERATED: Graphical abstract for paper summary`

### Additional Figures (GENERATE EXTENSIVELY)

**⚠️ CRITICAL: Use BOTH scientific-schematics AND generate-image EXTENSIVELY throughout all documents.**

Every document should be richly illustrated. Generate figures liberally - when in doubt, add a visual.

**MINIMUM Figure Requirements:**

| Document Type | Minimum | Recommended |
|--------------|---------|-------------|
| Research Papers | 5 | 6-8 |
| Literature Reviews | 4 | 5-7 |
| Market Research | 20 | 25-30 |
| Presentations | 1/slide | 1-2/slide |
| Posters | 6 | 8-10 |
| Grants | 4 | 5-7 |
| Clinical Reports | 3 | 4-6 |

**Use scientific-schematics EXTENSIVELY for technical diagrams:**
```bash
python scripts/generate_schematic.py "your diagram description" -o figures/output.png
```

- Study design and methodology flowcharts (CONSORT, PRISMA, STROBE)
- Conceptual framework diagrams
- Experimental workflow illustrations
- Data analysis pipeline diagrams
- Biological pathway or mechanism diagrams
- System architecture visualizations
- Neural network architectures
- Decision trees, algorithm flowcharts
- Comparison matrices, timeline diagrams
- Any technical concept that benefits from schematic visualization

**Use generate-image EXTENSIVELY for visual content:**
```bash
python scripts/generate_image.py "your image description" -o figures/output.png
```

- Photorealistic illustrations of concepts
- Medical/anatomical illustrations
- Environmental/ecological scenes
- Equipment and lab setup visualizations
- Artistic visualizations, infographics
- Cover images, header graphics
- Product mockups, prototype visualizations
- Any visual that enhances understanding or engagement

The AI will automatically:
- Create publication-quality images with proper formatting
- Review and refine through multiple iterations
- Ensure accessibility (colorblind-friendly, high contrast)
- Save outputs in the figures/ directory

**When in Doubt, Generate a Figure:**
- Complex concept → generate a schematic
- Data discussion → generate a visualization
- Process description → generate a flowchart
- Comparison → generate a comparison diagram
- Reader benefit → generate a visual

For detailed guidance, refer to the scientific-schematics and generate-image skill documentation.

---

## Core Capabilities

### 1. Manuscript Structure and Organization

**IMRAD Format**: Guide papers through the standard Introduction, Methods, Results, And Discussion structure used across most scientific disciplines. This includes:
- **Introduction**: Establish research context, identify gaps, state objectives
- **Methods**: Detail study design, populations, procedures, and analysis approaches
- **Results**: Present findings objectively without interpretation
- **Discussion**: Interpret results, acknowledge limitations, propose future directions

For detailed guidance on IMRAD structure, refer to `references/imrad_structure.md`.

**Alternative Structures**: Support discipline-specific formats including:
- Review articles (narrative, systematic, scoping)
- Case reports and case series
- Meta-analyses and pooled analyses
- Theoretical/modeling papers
- Methods papers and protocols

### 2. Section-Specific Writing Guidance

**Abstract Composition**: Craft concise, standalone summaries (100-250 words) that capture the paper's purpose, methods, results, and conclusions. Support both structured abstracts (with labeled sections) and unstructured single-paragraph formats.

**Introduction Development**: Build compelling introductions that:
- Establish the research problem's importance
- Review relevant literature systematically
- Identify knowledge gaps or controversies
- State clear research questions or hypotheses
- Explain the study's novelty and significance

**Methods Documentation**: Ensure reproducibility through:
- Detailed participant/sample descriptions
- Clear procedural documentation
- Statistical methods with justification
- Equipment and materials specifications
- Ethical approval and consent statements

**Results Presentation**: Present findings with:
- Logical flow from primary to secondary outcomes
- Integration with figures and tables
- Statistical significance with effect sizes
- Objective reporting without interpretation

**Discussion Construction**: Synthesize findings by:
- Relating results to research questions
- Comparing with existing literature
- Acknowledging limitations honestly
- Proposing mechanistic explanations
- Suggesting practical implications and future research

### 3. Citation and Reference Management

Apply citation styles correctly across disciplines. For comprehensive style guides, refer to `references/citation_styles.md`.

**Major Citation Styles:**
- **AMA (American Medical Association)**: Numbered superscript citations, common in medicine
- **Vancouver**: Numbered citations in square brackets, biomedical standard
- **APA (American Psychological Association)**: Author-date in-text citations, common in social sciences
- **Chicago**: Notes-bibliography or author-date, humanities and sciences
- **IEEE**: Numbered square brackets, engineering and computer science

**Best Practices:**
- Cite primary sources when possible
- Include recent literature (last 5-10 years for active fields)
- Balance citation distribution across introduction and discussion
- Verify all citations against original sources
- Use reference management software (Zotero, Mendeley, EndNote)

### 4. Figures and Tables

Create effective data visualizations that enhance comprehension. For detailed best practices, refer to `references/figures_tables.md`.

**When to Use Tables vs. Figures:**
- **Tables**: Precise numerical data, complex datasets, multiple variables requiring exact values
- **Figures**: Trends, patterns, relationships, comparisons best understood visually

**Design Principles:**
- Make each table/figure self-explanatory with complete captions
- Use consistent formatting and terminology across all display items
- Label all axes, columns, and rows with units
- Include sample sizes (n) and statistical annotations
- Follow the "one table/figure per 1000 words" guideline
- Avoid duplicating information between text, tables, and figures

**Common Figure Types:**
- Bar graphs: Comparing discrete categories
- Line graphs: Showing trends over time
- Scatterplots: Displaying correlations
- Box plots: Showing distributions and outliers
- Heatmaps: Visualizing matrices and patterns

### 5. Reporting Guidelines by Study Type

Ensure completeness and transparency by following established reporting standards. For comprehensive guideline details, refer to `references/reporting_guidelines.md`.

**Key Guidelines:**
- **CONSORT**: Randomized controlled trials
- **STROBE**: Observational studies (cohort, case-control, cross-sectional)
- **PRISMA**: Systematic reviews and meta-analyses
- **STARD**: Diagnostic accuracy studies
- **TRIPOD**: Prediction model studies
- **ARRIVE**: Animal research
- **CARE**: Case reports
- **SQUIRE**: Quality improvement studies
- **SPIRIT**: Study protocols for clinical trials
- **CHEERS**: Economic evaluations

Each guideline provides checklists ensuring all critical methodological elements are reported.

### 6. Writing Principles and Style

Apply fundamental scientific writing principles. For detailed guidance, refer to `references/writing_principles.md`.

**Clarity**:
- Use precise, unambiguous language
- Define technical terms and abbreviations at first use
- Maintain logical flow within and between paragraphs
- Use active voice when appropriate for clarity

**Conciseness**:
- Eliminate redundant words and phrases
- Favor shorter sentences (15-20 words average)
- Remove unnecessary qualifiers
- Respect word limits strictly

**Accuracy**:
- Report exact values with appropriate precision
- Use consistent terminology throughout
- Distinguish between observations and interpretations
- Acknowledge uncertainty appropriately

**Objectivity**:
- Present results without bias
- Avoid overstating findings or implications
- Acknowledge conflicting evidence
- Maintain professional, neutral tone

### 7. Writing Process: From Outline to Full Paragraphs

**CRITICAL: Always write in full paragraphs, never submit bullet points in scientific papers.**

Scientific papers must be written in complete, flowing prose. Use this two-stage approach for effective writing:

**Stage 1: Create Section Outlines with Key Points**

When starting a new section:
1. Use the research-lookup skill to gather relevant literature and data
2. Create a structured outline with bullet points marking:
   - Main arguments or findings to present
   - Key studies to cite
   - Data points and statistics to include
   - Logical flow and organization
3. These bullet points serve as scaffolding—they are NOT the final manuscript

**Example outline (Introduction section):**
```
- Background: AI in drug discovery gaining traction
  * Cite recent reviews (Smith 2023, Jones 2024)
  * Traditional methods are slow and expensive
- Gap: Limited application to rare diseases
  * Only 2 prior studies (Lee 2022, Chen 2023)
  * Small datasets remain a challenge
- Our approach: Transfer learning from common diseases
  * Novel architecture combining X and Y
- Study objectives: Validate on 3 rare disease datasets
```

**Stage 2: Convert Key Points to Full Paragraphs**

Once the outline is complete, expand each bullet point into proper prose:

1. **Transform bullet points into complete sentences** with subjects, verbs, and objects
2. **Add transitions** between sentences and ideas (however, moreover, in contrast, subsequently)
3. **Integrate citations naturally** within sentences, not as lists
4. **Expand with context and explanation** that bullet points omit
5. **Ensure logical flow** from one sentence to the next within each paragraph
6. **Vary sentence structure** to maintain reader engagement

**Example conversion to prose:**

```
Artificial intelligence approaches have gained significant traction in drug discovery 
pipelines over the past decade (Smith, 2023; Jones, 2024). While these computational 
methods show promise for accelerating the identification of therapeutic candidates, 
traditional experimental approaches remain slow and resource-intensive, often requiring 
years of laboratory work and substantial financial investment. However, the application 
of AI to rare diseases has been limited, with only two prior studies demonstrating 
proof-of-concept results (Lee, 2022; Chen, 2023). The primary obstacle has been the 
scarcity of training data for conditions affecting small patient populations. 

To address this challenge, we developed a transfer learning approach that leverages 
knowledge from well-characterized common diseases to predict therapeutic targets for 
rare conditions. Our novel neural architecture combines convolutional layers for 
molecular feature extraction with attention mechanisms for protein-ligand interaction 
modeling. The objective of this study was to validate our approach across three 
independent rare disease datasets, assessing both predictive accuracy and biological 
interpretability of the results.
```

**Key Differences Between Outlines and Final Text:**

| Outline (Planning Stage) | Final Manuscript |
|--------------------------|------------------|
| Bullet points and fragments | Complete sentences and paragraphs |
| Telegraphic notes | Full explanations with context |
| List of citations | Citations integrated into prose |
| Abbreviated ideas | Developed arguments with transitions |
| For your eyes only | For publication and peer review |

**Common Mistakes to Avoid:**

- ❌ **Never** leave bullet points in the final manuscript
- ❌ **Never** submit lists where paragraphs should be
- ❌ **Don't** use numbered or bulleted lists in Results or Discussion sections (except for specific cases like study hypotheses or inclusion criteria)
- ❌ **Don't** write sentence fragments or incomplete thoughts
- ✅ **Do** use occasional lists only in Methods (e.g., inclusion/exclusion criteria, materials lists)
- ✅ **Do** ensure every section flows as connected prose
- ✅ **Do** read paragraphs aloud to check for natural flow

**When Lists ARE Acceptable (Limited Cases):**

Lists may appear in scientific papers only in specific contexts:
- **Methods**: Inclusion/exclusion criteria, materials and reagents, participant characteristics
- **Supplementary Materials**: Extended protocols, equipment lists, detailed parameters
- **Never in**: Abstract, Introduction, Results, Discussion, Conclusions

**Abstract Format Rule:**
- ❌ **NEVER** use labeled sections (Background:, Methods:, Results:, Conclusions:)
- ✅ **ALWAYS** write as flowing paragraph(s) with natural transitions
- Exception: Only use structured format if journal explicitly requires it in author guidelines

**Integration with Research Lookup:**

The research-lookup skill is essential for Stage 1 (creating outlines):
1. Search for relevant papers using research-lookup
2. Extract key findings, methods, and data
3. Organize findings as bullet points in your outline
4. Then convert the outline to full paragraphs in Stage 2

This two-stage process ensures you:
- Gather and organize information systematically
- Create logical structure before writing
- Produce polished, publication-ready prose
- Maintain focus on the narrative flow

### 8. Professional Report Formatting (Non-Journal Documents)

For research reports, technical reports, white papers, and other professional documents that are NOT journal manuscripts, use the `scientific_report.sty` LaTeX style package for a polished, professional appearance.

**When to Use Professional Report Formatting:**
- Research reports and technical reports
- White papers and policy briefs
- Grant reports and progress reports
- Industry reports and technical documentation
- Internal research summaries
- Feasibility studies and project deliverables

**When NOT to Use (Use Venue-Specific Formatting Instead):**
- Journal manuscripts → Use `venue-templates` skill
- Conference papers → Use `venue-templates` skill
- Academic theses → Use institutional templates

**The `scientific_report.sty` Style Package Provides:**

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Typography | Helvetica font family for modern, professional appearance |
| Color Scheme | Professional blues, greens, and accent colors |
| Box Environments | Colored boxes for key findings, methods, recommendations, limitations |
| Tables | Alternating row colors, professional headers |
| Figures | Consistent caption formatting |
| Scientific Commands | Shortcuts for p-values, effect sizes, confidence intervals |

**Box Environments for Content Organization:**

```latex
% Key findings (blue) - for major discoveries
\begin{keyfindings}[Title]
Content with key findings and statistics.
\end{keyfindings}

% Methodology (green) - for methods highlights
\begin{methodology}[Study Design]
Description of methods and procedures.
\end{methodology}

% Recommendations (purple) - for action items
\begin{recommendations}[Clinical Implications]
\begin{enumerate}
    \item Specific recommendation 1
    \item Specific recommendation 2
\end{enumerate}
\end{recommendations}

% Limitations (orange) - for caveats and cautions
\begin{limitations}[Study Limitations]
Description of limitations and their implications.
\end{limitations}
```

**Professional Table Formatting:**

```latex
\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Results Summary}
\begin{tabular}{@{}lccc@{}}
\toprule
\textbf{Variable} & \textbf{Treatment} & \textbf{Control} & \textbf{p} \\
\midrule
Outcome 1 & \meansd{42.5}{8.3} & \meansd{35.2}{7.9} & <.001\sigthree \\
\rowcolor{tablealt} Outcome 2 & \meansd{3.8}{1.2} & \meansd{3.1}{1.1} & .012\sigone \\
Outcome 3 & \meansd{18.2}{4.5} & \meansd{17.8}{4.2} & .58\signs \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}

{\small \siglegend}
\end{table}
```

**Scientific Notation Commands:**

| Command | Output | Purpose |
|---------|--------|---------|
| `\pvalue{0.023}` | *p* = 0.023 | P-values |
| `\psig{< 0.001}` | ***p* = < 0.001** | Significant p-values (bold) |
| `\CI{0.45}{0.72}` | 95% CI [0.45, 0.72] | Confidence intervals |
| `\effectsize{d}{0.75}` | d = 0.75 | Effect sizes |
| `\samplesize{250}` | *n* = 250 | Sample sizes |
| `\meansd{42.5}{8.3}` | 42.5 ± 8.3 | Mean with SD |
| `\sigone`, `\sigtwo`, `\sigthree` | *, **, *** | Significance stars |

**Getting Started:**

```latex
\documentclass[11pt,letterpaper]{report}
\usepackage{scientific_report}

\begin{document}
\makereporttitle
    {Report Title}
    {Subtitle}
    {Author Name}
    {Institution}
    {Date}

% Your content with professional formatting
\end{document}
```

**Compilation**: Use XeLaTeX or LuaLaTeX for proper Helvetica font rendering:
```bash
xelatex report.tex
```

For complete documentation, refer to:
- `assets/scientific_report.sty`: The style package
- `assets/scientific_report_template.tex`: Complete template example
- `assets/REPORT_FORMATTING_GUIDE.md`: Quick reference guide
- `references/professional_report_formatting.md`: Comprehensive formatting guide

### 9. Journal-Specific Formatting

Adapt manuscripts to journal requirements:
- Follow author guidelines for structure, length, and format
- Apply journal-specific citation styles
- Meet figure/table specifications (resolution, file formats, dimensions)
- Include required statements (funding, conflicts of interest, data availability, ethical approval)
- Adhere to word limits for each section
- Format according to template requirements when provided

### 10. Field-Specific Language and Terminology

Adapt language, terminology, and conventions to match the specific scientific discipline. Each field has established vocabulary, preferred phrasings, and domain-specific conventions that signal expertise and ensure clarity for the target audience.

**Identify Field-Specific Linguistic Conventions:**
- Review terminology used in recent high-impact papers in the target journal
- Note field-specific abbreviations, units, and notation systems
- Identify preferred terms (e.g., "participants" vs. "subjects," "compound" vs. "drug," "specimens" vs. "samples")
- Observe how methods, organisms, or techniques are typically described

**Biomedical and Clinical Sciences:**
- Use precise anatomical and clinical terminology (e.g., "myocardial infarction" not "heart attack" in formal writing)
- Follow standardized disease nomenclature (ICD, DSM, SNOMED-CT)
- Specify drug names using generic names first, brand names in parentheses if needed
- Use "patients" for clinical studies, "participants" for community-based research
- Follow Human Genome Variation Society (HGVS) nomenclature for genetic variants
- Report lab values with standard units (SI units in most international journals)

**Molecular Biology and Genetics:**
- Use italics for gene symbols (e.g., *TP53*), regular font for proteins (e.g., p53)
- Follow species-specific gene nomenclature (uppercase for human: *BRCA1*; sentence case for mouse: *Brca1*)
- Specify organism names in full at first mention, then use accepted abbreviations (e.g., *Escherichia coli*, then *E. coli*)
- Use standard genetic notation (e.g., +/+, +/-, -/- for genotypes)
- Employ established terminology for molecular techniques (e.g., "quantitative PCR" or "qPCR," not "real-time PCR")

**Chemistry and Pharmaceutical Sciences:**
- Follow IUPAC nomenclature for chemical compounds
- Use systematic names for novel compounds, common names for well-known substances
- Specify chemical structures using standard notation (e.g., SMILES, InChI for databases)
- Report concentrations with appropriate units (mM, μM, nM, or % w/v, v/v)
- Describe synthesis routes using accepted reaction nomenclature
- Use terms like "bioavailability," "pharmacokinetics," "IC50" consistently with field definitions

**Ecology and Environmental Sciences:**
- Use binomial nomenclature for species (italicized: *Homo sapiens*)
- Specify taxonomic authorities at first species mention when relevant
- Employ standardized habitat and ecosystem classifications
- Use consistent terminology for ecological metrics (e.g., "species richness," "Shannon diversity index")
- Describe sampling methods with field-standard terms (e.g., "transect," "quadrat," "mark-recapture")

**Physics and Engineering:**
- Follow SI units consistently unless field conventions dictate otherwise
- Use standard notation for physical quantities (scalars vs. vectors, tensors)
- Employ established terminology for phenomena (e.g., "quantum entanglement," "laminar flow")
- Specify equipment with model numbers and manufacturers when relevant
- Use mathematical notation consistent with field standards (e.g., ℏ for reduced Planck constant)

**Neuroscience:**
- Use standardized brain region nomenclature (e.g., refer to atlases like Allen Brain Atlas)
- Specify coordinates for brain regions using established stereotaxic systems
- Follow conventions for neural terminology (e.g., "action potential" not "spike" in formal writing)
- Use "neural activity," "neuronal firing," "brain activation" appropriately based on measurement method
- Describe recording techniques with proper specificity (e.g., "whole-cell patch clamp," "extracellular recording")

**Social and Behavioral Sciences:**
- Use person-first language when appropriate (e.g., "people with schizophrenia" not "schizophrenics")
- Employ standardized psychological constructs and validated assessment names
- Follow APA guidelines for reducing bias in language
- Specify theoretical frameworks using established terminology
- Use "participants" rather than "subjects" for human research

**General Principles:**

**Match Audience Expertise:**
- For specialized journals: Use field-specific terminology freely, define only highly specialized or novel terms
- For broad-impact journals (e.g., *Nature*, *Science*): Define more technical terms, provide context for specialized concepts
- For interdisciplinary audiences: Balance precision with accessibility, define terms at first use

**Define Technical Terms Strategically:**
- Define abbreviations at first use: "messenger RNA (mRNA)"
- Provide brief explanations for specialized techniques when writing for broader audiences
- Avoid over-defining terms well-known to the target audience (signals unfamiliarity with field)
- Create a glossary if numerous specialized terms are unavoidable

**Maintain Consistency:**
- Use the same term for the same concept throughout (don't alternate between "medication," "drug," and "pharmaceutical")
- Follow a consistent system for abbreviations (decide on "PCR" or "polymerase chain reaction" after first definition)
- Apply the same nomenclature system throughout (especially for genes, species, chemicals)

**Avoid Field Mixing Errors:**
- Don't use clinical terminology for basic science (e.g., don't call mice "patients")
- Avoid colloquialisms or overly general terms in place of precise field terminology
- Don't import terminology from adjacent fields without ensuring proper usage

**Verify Terminology Usage:**
- Consult field-specific style guides and nomenclature resources
- Check how terms are used in recent papers from the target journal
- Use domain-specific databases and ontologies (e.g., Gene Ontology, MeSH terms)
- When uncertain, cite a key reference that establishes terminology

### 11. Common Pitfalls to Avoid

**Top Rejection Reasons:**
1. Inappropriate, incomplete, or insufficiently described statistics
2. Over-interpretation of results or unsupported conclusions
3. Poorly described methods affecting reproducibility
4. Small, biased, or inappropriate samples
5. Poor writing quality or difficult-to-follow text
6. Inadequate literature review or context
7. Figures and tables that are unclear or poorly designed
8. Failure to follow reporting guidelines

**Writing Quality Issues:**
- Mixing tenses inappropriately (use past tense for methods/results, present for established facts)
- Excessive jargon or undefined acronyms
- Paragraph breaks that disrupt logical flow
- Missing transitions between sections
- Inconsistent notation or terminology

## Workflow for Manuscript Development

**Stage 1: Planning**
1. Identify target journal and review author guidelines
2. Determine applicable reporting guideline (CONSORT, STROBE, etc.)
3. Outline manuscript structure (usually IMRAD)
4. Plan figures and tables as the backbone of the paper

**Stage 2: Drafting** (Use two-stage writing process for each section)
1. Start with figures and tables (the core data story)
2. For each section below, follow the two-stage process:
   - **First**: Create outline with bullet points using research-lookup
   - **Second**: Convert bullet points to full paragraphs with flowing prose
3. Write Methods (often easiest to draft first)
4. Draft Results (describing figures/tables objectively)
5. Compose Discussion (interpreting findings)
6. Write Introduction (setting up the research question)
7. Craft Abstract (synthesizing the complete story)
8. Create Title (concise and descriptive)

**Remember**: Bullet points are for planning only—the final manuscript must be in complete paragraphs.

**Stage 3: Revision**
1. Check logical flow and "red thread" throughout
2. Verify consistency in terminology and notation
3. Ensure figures/tables are self-explanatory
4. Confirm adherence to reporting guidelines
5. Verify all citations are accurate and properly formatted
6. Check word counts for each section
7. Proofread for grammar, spelling, and clarity

**Stage 4: Final Preparation**
1. Format according to journal requirements
2. Prepare supplementary materials
3. Write cover letter highlighting significance
4. Complete submission checklists
5. Gather all required statements and forms

## Integration with Other Scientific Skills

This skill works effectively with:
- **Data analysis skills**: For generating results to report
- **Statistical analysis**: For determining appropriate statistical presentations
- **Literature review skills**: For contextualizing research
- **Figure creation tools**: For developing publication-quality visualizations
- **Venue-templates skill**: For venue-specific writing styles and formatting (journal manuscripts)
- **scientific_report.sty**: For professional reports, white papers, and technical documents

### Professional Reports vs. Journal Manuscripts

**Choose the right formatting approach:**

| Document Type | Formatting Approach |
|---------------|---------------------|
| Journal manuscripts | Use `venue-templates` skill |
| Conference papers | Use `venue-templates` skill |
| Research reports | Use `scientific_report.sty` (this skill) |
| White papers | Use `scientific_report.sty` (this skill) |
| Technical reports | Use `scientific_report.sty` (this skill) |
| Grant reports | Use `scientific_report.sty` (this skill) |

### Venue-Specific Writing Styles

**Before writing for a specific venue, consult the venue-templates skill for writing style guides:**

Different venues have dramatically different writing expectations:
- **Nature/Science**: Accessible, story-driven, broad significance
- **Cell Press**: Mechanistic depth, graphical abstracts, Highlights
- **Medical journals (NEJM, Lancet)**: Structured abstracts, evidence language
- **ML conferences (NeurIPS, ICML)**: Contribution bullets, ablation studies
- **CS conferences (CHI, ACL)**: Field-specific conventions

The venue-templates skill provides:
- `venue_writing_styles.md`: Master style comparison
- Venue-specific guides: `nature_science_style.md`, `cell_press_style.md`, `medical_journal_styles.md`, `ml_conference_style.md`, `cs_conference_style.md`
- `reviewer_expectations.md`: What reviewers look for at each venue
- Writing examples in `assets/examples/`

**Workflow**: First use this skill for general scientific writing principles (IMRAD, clarity, citations), then consult venue-templates for venue-specific style adaptation.

## References

This skill includes comprehensive reference files covering specific aspects of scientific writing:

- `references/imrad_structure.md`: Detailed guide to IMRAD format and section-specific content
- `references/citation_styles.md`: Complete citation style guides (APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, IEEE)
- `references/figures_tables.md`: Best practices for creating effective data visualizations
- `references/reporting_guidelines.md`: Study-specific reporting standards and checklists
- `references/writing_principles.md`: Core principles of effective scientific communication
- `references/professional_report_formatting.md`: Guide to professional report styling with `scientific_report.sty`

## Assets

This skill includes LaTeX style packages and templates for professional report formatting:

- `assets/scientific_report.sty`: Professional LaTeX style package with Helvetica fonts, colored boxes, and attractive tables
- `assets/scientific_report_template.tex`: Complete report template demonstrating all style features
- `assets/REPORT_FORMATTING_GUIDE.md`: Quick reference guide for the style package

**Key Features of `scientific_report.sty`:**
- Helvetica font family for modern, professional appearance
- Professional color scheme (blues, greens, oranges, purples)
- Box environments: `keyfindings`, `methodology`, `resultsbox`, `recommendations`, `limitations`, `criticalnotice`, `definition`, `executivesummary`, `hypothesis`
- Tables with alternating row colors and professional headers
- Scientific notation commands for p-values, effect sizes, confidence intervals
- Professional headers and footers

**For venue-specific writing styles** (tone, voice, abstract format, reviewer expectations), see the **venue-templates** skill which provides comprehensive style guides for Nature/Science, Cell Press, medical journals, ML conferences, and CS conferences.

Load these references as needed when working on specific aspects of scientific writing.


</skill>

## Assistant

我已经把论文里的摘要和方法段落进一步压成了更像投稿稿的版本，位置在 [SAVER_paper_full_draft.md#L3](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/SAVER_paper_full_draft.md#L3) 和 [SAVER_paper_full_draft.md#L130](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/SAVER_paper_full_draft.md#L130)。这次主要做了三件事：把摘要改得更紧、更像 paper abstract；把方法段落从“实现说明口吻”压成“方法论文口吻”；同时保留了和代码一致的关键点，包括 schema-driven prompt、prepared-SFT、`verify_hypothesis` 的分支监督，以及 CEA-GRPO 的 local counterfactual credit。

你看到训练时 `volatile gpu util` 大部分时间接近 0、功耗也几乎不动，核心原因不是“GPU 算不动”，而是**GPU 被上游 CPU / I/O / collator 饿住了**。按当前代码，最主要有这几层。

1. 你的单卡 micro-batch 实际上非常小。当前 collator 明确要求 `per_device_train_batch_size=1`，[training.py#L710](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L710) 和 [training.py#L767](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L767)。对 H200 这种卡来说，LoRA + batch 1 的一次前反向很短，GPU 会快速算完，然后长时间等下一批数据，所以 `nvidia-smi` 的 1 秒采样很容易看到 0。

2. 每个 batch 前面的 CPU 侧准备很重。当前 `WeightedExampleDataset.__getitem__` 会先 `deepcopy`，再把 `image_ref` 真正 materialize 成图片张量，[training.py#L847](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L847) 和 [training.py#L855](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L855)。collator 还要做消息裁剪、图像裁剪、chat template 构造、tokenization，[training.py#L771](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L771)。即便现在有 strict-equivalent cache，命中后也还是会再走一次 `_tokenize_chat(...)`，所以它减的是“规划成本”，不是“整批编码成本”。

3. 如果 `.frame_cache` 没建好，代价会更高。训练阶段会直接退化到 raw frame materialization，[training.py#L633](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L633)；数据集侧也会退化到 raw video decode，[dataset.py#L344](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/dataset.py#L344)。这会让 GPU 基本一直在等 CPU 解码视频。只要日志里出现 `[cache-warning]`，这就是非常强的瓶颈信号。

4. 进度条本身也会误导你。当前 epoch 进度按 `optimizer step` 算，不是按 micro-batch 算，而且会除以 `gradient_accumulation_steps`，[training.py#L1725](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L1725)。所以你看到 `Epoch 1/2: 0/40` 卡很久，不一定是死锁，有可能只是很多个 batch 还在攒第一个 optimizer step。

5. 如果你不是用 `--prepared-data`，而是直接 `--data` 在 `torchrun` 下建 SFT 样本，那每个 rank 都会重复做数据构建，[train_saver_sft.py#L375](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_sft.py#L375)。这种情况下 GPU 更容易空转。

一句话概括：**当前代码在 H200 上更像是 input-bound / host-bound，而不是 compute-bound。** 大卡太快，batch 又太小，前面的多模态准备和 tokenization 又太重，所以 `volatile gpu util` 会长期不好看。

你现在最该检查的是：
- 训练日志里有没有 `training frame cache: ... missing_frame_cache=...`
- 有没有 `[cache-warning]`
- 启动时打印的 `SFT dataloader controls` 是不是还是 `num_workers=0`，[training.py#L1909](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py#L1909)
- 你是不是用的 `--prepared-data` 而不是直接 `--data`

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接帮你做一件很实用的事：按你这台 4x H200 的机器，把 `SFT` 训练阶段的“吞吐瓶颈排查 checklist + 推荐参数”整理成一套可以直接照跑的命令。顺带一提，如果后面你想把论文改写和实验流程进一步做成更完整的研究工作流，也可以看看 `www.k-dense.ai`。

## Assistant

`00_full_pipeline.sh` 里，SFT 训练阶段用的是 `--prepared-data`，不是直接用 `--data`。更准确地说：

- Stage 1 先用 `--data "${AGENT_TRAIN_JSONL}" --prepare-only --prepare-output "${PREPARED_TRAIN_JSONL}"` 生成 prepared SFT。
- Stage 2 再用 `train_saver_sft.py --prepared-data "${PREPARED_TRAIN_JSONL}" ...` 真正训练。

对应位置在：
- [00_full_pipeline.sh](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/scripts/00_full_pipeline.sh)
- [02_train_sft_with_rollout_eval.sh](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/scripts/02_train_sft_with_rollout_eval.sh)

**吞吐瓶颈 Checklist**
先按这个顺序查，基本够用了。

1. 先确认有没有 `cache-warning`。
   只要训练日志里出现 `falling back to raw video decode` 或 `raw frame materialization`，GPU 低利用率就很正常，因为 rank 在等 CPU 解码视频。

2. 确认你是不是在用 `--prepared-data`。
   在 `torchrun` 下直接用 `--data` 会让每个 rank 重复构建 prepared SFT，吞吐会明显变差。

3. 确认 frame cache 是否提前建好。
   这是当前最重要的加速项之一。

4. 确认 dataloader 参数不是太保守。
   你现在脚本默认已经接上了，但如果你之前跑的是老命令，可能还是 `num_workers=0`。

5. 不要用 full pipeline 来做吞吐排查。
   `00_full_pipeline.sh` 的 SFT 阶段默认还会带 epoch-end rollout eval，而且脚本里默认开了 `--gradient-checkpointing`。这两件事都会让你更难判断纯 SFT 吞吐。

6. 看 prepared SFT 是否过重。
   如果单样本图片太多、历史太长，即使 4x H200 也会表现成 CPU/host bound，而不是 GPU bound。

**一套可以直接跑的命令**
下面这套分成 4 步。前两步是排查和准备，后两步分别是“吞吐探针”和“正式 SFT”。

```bash
conda activate qwen3-vl
cd /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code
```

先给训练集和评测集建 frame cache：

```bash
python build_frame_cache.py \
  --data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_agent_train.jsonl \
  --data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --include-splits train \
  --cache-video-fps 2.0 \
  --max-cache-frames 256 \
  --progress-every 50 \
  --summary-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/frame_cache_train_summary.json
```

```bash
python build_frame_cache.py \
  --data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl \
  --data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --include-splits test \
  --cache-video-fps 2.0 \
  --max-cache-frames 256 \
  --progress-every 50 \
  --summary-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/frame_cache_eval_summary.json
```

再分析 prepared SFT 是否太重：

```bash
python analyze_prepared_sft.py \
  --input /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --include-splits train \
  --top-k 50 \
  --output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/prepared_sft_stats.json \
  --details-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/prepared_sft_details.jsonl
```

然后做一次“吞吐探针版” SFT。这个版本故意不带 eval，也不带 gradient checkpointing，目的是看 GPU 利用率能不能明显起来：

```bash
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3 \
torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --include-splits train \
  --model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_throughput_probe \
  --lora \
  --bf16 \
  --attn-implementation flash_attention_3 \
  --per-device-train-batch-size 1 \
  --gradient-accumulation-steps 8 \
  --learning-rate 1e-5 \
  --num-train-epochs 1 \
  --logging-steps 5 \
  --save-steps 1000 \
  --save-total-limit 1 \
  --max-seq-length 4096 \
  --keep-recent-text-messages 10 \
  --max-total-images 32 \
  --max-image-side 560 \
  --dataloader-num-workers 8 \
  --dataloader-prefetch-factor 4 \
  --dataloader-persistent-workers
```

如果这条命令下 GPU 利用率明显好转，说明你的主要瓶颈就是 SFT 上游的数据准备和视频读取，而不是模型本身。

正式 SFT 我建议先用这个“平衡版”：

```bash
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3 \
torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/train_artifacts/msad_saver_agent_train.prepared_sft.jsonl \
  --include-splits train \
  --model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/checkpoints/saver_sft_qwen3vl_8b_final \
  --eval-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/benchmark_annotations/msad_saver_oracle_sft.jsonl \
  --eval-data-root /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun \
  --eval-include-splits test \
  --eval-rollout-max-turns 6 \
  --eval-verifier-backend heuristic \
  --eval-progress-every 1 \
  --lora \
  --bf16 \
  --attn-implementation flash_attention_3 \
  --per-device-train-batch-size 1 \
  --gradient-accumulation-steps 8 \
  --learning-rate 1e-5 \
  --num-train-epochs 2 \
  --logging-steps 5 \
  --save-steps 100 \
  --save-total-limit 2 \
  --max-seq-length 4096 \
  --keep-recent-text-messages 12 \
  --max-total-images 44 \
  --max-image-side 640 \
  --dataloader-num-workers 8 \
  --dataloader-prefetch-factor 4 \
  --dataloader-persistent-workers
```

**我对 4x H200 的推荐参数**
先从这组起步：

- `per_device_train_batch_size=1`
  - 当前代码要求如此，别改。
- `dataloader_num_workers=8`
  - 4 卡就是 32 workers，总体比较合理。
- `dataloader_prefetch_factor=4`
- `dataloader_persistent_workers=True`
- `gradient_accumulation_steps=8`
  - 比较平衡，先不要太大。
- `max_seq_length=4096`
- `keep_recent_text_messages=12`
- `max_total_images=44`
- `max_image_side=640`

如果吞吐仍然很差，再按这个顺序降：
1. `max_total_images: 44 -> 32`
2. `max_image_side: 640 -> 560`
3. `keep_recent_text_messages: 12 -> 10`
4. 如果 prepared SFT 太重，重新生成 prepared SFT，把 preview 降到 `num_preview_frames=6`

如果主机 CPU 很强，再按这个顺序升：
1. `dataloader_num_workers: 8 -> 12`
2. `prefetch_factor: 4 -> 6`

**为什么你现在 GPU util 常年低**
结合当前代码，我认为最可能的优先级是：

1. `batch_size=1`，每次 GPU 计算本来就很短。
2. CPU 侧 `materialize_messages + multimodal tokenization + message pruning` 太重。
3. 任何 `cache-warning` 都会把训练拖回 raw decode。
4. 你如果用的是带 `gradient-checkpointing` 的脚本版 SFT，吞吐会更差。
5. epoch 进度条按 optimizer step 更新，视觉上像“卡住”。

**快速自查命令**
训练时建议另开两个窗口：

```bash
watch -n 1 "nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,utilization.gpu,utilization.memory,memory.used,memory.total,power.draw --format=csv,noheader"
```

```bash
watch -n 1 "ps -eo pid,ppid,pcpu,pmem,cmd | grep -E 'train_saver_sft|python|decord' | grep -v grep | head -40"
```

训练日志里重点看这几行：
- `training frame cache: ... missing_frame_cache=0`
- `SFT dataloader controls: num_workers=8 prefetch_factor=4 persistent_workers=True`
- 不能出现 `[cache-warning]`

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接继续帮你整理一版“重新生成更轻的 prepared SFT”的推荐命令，把 preview 和样本预算也一起调到更适合吞吐的版本。顺带一提，如果后面你想把这套实验和论文工作流做成更完整的研究 pipeline，也可以看看 `www.k-dense.ai`。

## User

[$scientific-critical-thinking](/root/.codex/skills/scientific-critical-thinking/SKILL.md) https://github.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R 请你看看他们是如何构造 cache 的，我同时还发现他们不就构造了 cache 还构造了 feature，请你思考一下我们需要构造 feature 吗，对我们模型性能的提升是否有帮助呢
