## User

关于 verifier，当前默认情况是：

  - SFT 的 epoch-end eval：heuristic
  - score_saver_rollout.py：默认 heuristic
  - RL 在线 verify_hypothesis：默认 hybrid
  - hybrid 里用的 verifier-model-path，默认是原始 qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct

  所以如果你没手工改参数，当前 RL 在线 verifier 确实是：

  - 原始 Qwen 为辅  这里提到的规则 verifier 是什么东西

## Assistant

我直接看 `verifier.py` 里的 `heuristic` 实现，把“规则 verifier”拆成输入、判断逻辑和输出三部分说明清楚。

## Assistant

这里的“规则 verifier”不是另一个模型，而是一套写死在代码里的启发式判定逻辑，核心在 [verifier.py:580](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L580)。

它做的事可以理解成 4 步：

1. 先取当前要验证的证据子集  
来自当前 `state.evidence_ledger / visited_windows`，以及你传进去的 `candidate_window_ids / candidate_evidence_ids / candidate_evidence_moment_ids`，见 [verifier.py:139](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L139)。

2. 再构造 4 个“视角”打分  
它不是只看“当前保留的证据”，而是同时看：
- `full`：全部证据
- `keep`：只保留当前选中的证据
- `drop`：把当前选中的证据拿掉后剩下的证据
- `alert_prefix`：只看 alert 时间点之前的证据  
这些是在 [verifier.py:635](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L635) 开始组出来的。

3. 每个视角算一组 support 分数  
例如：
- `exist_support`
- `category_support`
- `temporal_support`
- `precursor_support`
- `alert_support`
- `counterfactual_support`  
这些分数怎么来，取决于有没有参考监督：

- 有 GT 参考时，它会用 oracle windows / structured target，按时间重叠和角色覆盖来算，见 [verifier.py:292](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L292)。
- 没有 GT 时，它会退化成 fallback heuristic，比如看覆盖了多少视频、看了几个窗口，见 [verifier.py:204](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L204) 和 [verifier.py:260](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L260)。

4. 最后把分数压成离散结论  
它会先算几个反事实量：
- `sufficiency = keep_score`
- `necessity = full_score - drop_score`
- `counterfactual_faithfulness`  
见 [verifier.py:377](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L377)。

然后再阈值化成：
- `primary_status`: `complete / incomplete / redundant / misaligned`，见 [verifier.py:493](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L493)
- `alert_status`: `justified / premature / late / not_applicable`，见 [verifier.py:519](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L519)
- `recommended_action`: `continue_search / revise_claim / refine_evidence / finalize`，见 [verifier.py:548](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L548)

所以一句话概括：

规则 verifier = 一个纯 Python 的、基于时间窗覆盖/角色重叠/反事实 keep-drop 逻辑的判分器，不是神经网络模型。

你前面看到的 `hybrid`，就是：
- 先跑这套规则 verifier
- 再跑一次 Qwen self-verifier
- 最后按权重混合，见 [verifier.py:469](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L469) 和 [verifier.py:683](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L683)

默认 `alpha=0.7`，意思通常是：
- 70% 信规则 verifier
- 30% 信 Qwen verifier

如果你愿意，我下一条可以继续把这套规则 verifier 用一个具体视频案例，从 `keep/drop/full` 三个视角给你逐步演示一遍。

把这套规则 verifier 用一个具体视频案例，从 keep/drop/full 三个视角给你逐步演示一遍。

## Assistant

我用现有代码结构做一个最贴近真实运行的例子，把 `full / keep / drop` 三个视角和最终状态判定逐步拆开。

## Assistant

我把这个示例所在的测试行号取出来，下面会直接按这些对象解释，不空口说。

## Assistant

我用代码单测里的一个最标准案例来讲。这个案例不是某个真实文件名的视频，而是 verifier 的最小化“攻击异常”样本，定义在 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L36](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L36) 和 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L60](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L60)。

**案例设置**
这个视频被假定为一个 `assault` 异常，目标区间是 `[1.0, 8.0]`，最早预警时间是 `1.0s`，并且 oracle 里有 3 个关键证据窗口：
- `w0001 = [1,4]`，角色是 `trigger`
- `w0002 = [4,6]`，角色是 `peak_action`
- `w0003 = [6,8]`，角色是 `confirmation`

这些定义在 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L41](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L41) 到 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L55](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L55)，以及 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L61](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L61) 到 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L101](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L101)。

**先理解 `full / keep / drop`**
在 verifier 里这 3 个视角是这样来的，见 [verifier.py#L299](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L299) 到 [verifier.py#L394](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L394)：

- `full`
当前状态里“你一共搜到过的全部证据窗口”。
- `keep`
你这次声称“真正支撑当前 claim 的那一小撮证据”。
- `drop`
把 `keep` 从 `full` 里删掉后，剩下的证据。

然后每个视角各自算一组 support 分数：
- `exist_support`
- `category_support`
- `temporal_support`
- `alert_support`
- `counterfactual_support`
- 最后合成为 `overall_support`

核心规则在 [verifier.py#L331](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L331) 到 [verifier.py#L374](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L374)。

之后再派生出：
- `sufficiency = keep.overall_support`
- `necessity = full.overall_support - drop.overall_support`
- `consistency = 1 - |full - keep|`
见 [verifier.py#L378](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L378) 到 [verifier.py#L393](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L393)。

**例子 1：为什么 `keep = {w0001,w0002}` 会被判成 `complete`**
这就是单测 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L200](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L200)。

这里：
- `full = {w0001,w0002,w0003}`
- `keep = {w0001,w0002}`
- `drop = {w0003}`

直觉上：
- `full` 很强，因为 trigger、peak、confirmation 全都有。
- `keep` 也很强，因为它至少包含 `trigger + peak_action`，已经足够支持“这里确实发生了 assault，而且证据比较扎实”。
- `drop` 只剩 confirmation，说明“把 keep 拿掉以后，剩下的证据明显弱很多”。

按代码规则大致会变成：
- `keep.overall_support` 高，说明“只保留这部分证据，结论还能站住”
- `drop.overall_support` 明显下降，说明“把这部分证据拿掉，结论会塌很多”
- 所以 `sufficiency` 高，`necessity` 也高

于是命中 [verifier.py#L510](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L510) 到 [verifier.py#L515](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L515) 的条件：
- `keep >= 0.60`
- `necessity >= 0.20`
- `consistency >= 0.75`

最后输出：
- `primary_status = complete`
- `recommended_action = finalize`

**例子 2：为什么 `keep = {w0003}` 会被判成 `incomplete`**
这就是单测 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L217](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L217)。

这里：
- `keep = {w0003}`，只有 confirmation
- 也就是你只拿了“后果/确认”这一段，却没有拿到真正决定性的 `trigger` 或 `peak`

直觉上：
- `full` 还是强的，因为全证据在那
- 但 `keep` 太弱，只靠 confirmation 不足以单独支撑当前异常 claim
- 所以 `sufficiency` 低

按代码规则，会命中 [verifier.py#L506](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L506) 到 [verifier.py#L507](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L507)：
- `keep.overall_support < 0.55`
- 但 `full.overall_support >= 0.60`

意思就是：
“你明明已经搜到足够证据了，但你当前挑出来保留的这部分还不够。”

所以输出：
- `primary_status = incomplete`
- `recommended_action = continue_search`

这正对应 SAVER 想要的语义：不是“答案错了”，而是“当前选中的证据子集还不够支撑你的 claim”。

**例子 3：为什么 `keep = {w0001}` 会被判成 `redundant`**
这就是单测 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L232](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L232)。

这里：
- `keep = {w0001}`，只有 trigger
- `drop = {w0002,w0003}`，剩下 peak 和 confirmation

直觉上：
- `keep` 本身不算弱，因为 trigger 很关键
- 但问题是，把它拿掉以后，`drop` 仍然很强
- 换句话说，这个 `keep` 不是“必要证据”，因为不用它，结论照样还能成立得差不多

按代码规则，会命中 [verifier.py#L508](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L508) 到 [verifier.py#L509](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L509)：
- `keep.overall_support >= 0.60`
- `drop.overall_support >= 0.65`

这表示：
“你保留的这部分证据不是没用，但它不够必要，删掉以后剩余证据仍然太强。”

所以输出：
- `primary_status = redundant`
- `recommended_action = refine_evidence`

**顺手说一下 alert 为什么也能一起判**
除了 `full / keep / drop`，代码还会多算一个 `alert_prefix`，也就是“只看 alert 时间点之前能拿到的证据”，见 [verifier.py#L382](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L382) 和 [verifier.py#L519](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L519)。

所以：
- 如果你在 `1.2s` 报警，此时前缀里已经覆盖到 `w0001`，`alertability` 够高，就会是 `justified`
- 如果你在 `0.4s` 就报警，这时前缀里几乎没有可用证据，就会是 `premature`

这对应单测 [test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L247](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/tests/test_saver_agent_verifier.py#L247)。

**一句话总结**
这套规则 verifier 的核心不是“最终答案对不对”，而是：

- `keep` 看“只保留这些证据，结论还能不能站住”
- `drop` 看“把这些证据拿掉，结论会不会塌”
- `full` 看“你其实总共已经搜到了多少有效信息”

然后把这三者组合成：
- `complete`：既够用，又必要
- `incomplete`：不够用
- `redundant`：够用，但不够必要
- `misaligned`：根本没对上 claim

如果你愿意，我下一条可以继续把这个例子再翻成“真实 rollout 里 policy 调 `verify_hypothesis` 时，state 里到底传了什么字段”，把 `candidate_window_ids / evidence_ledger / alerts / verification_records` 全部串起来讲。复杂研究流程如果后面要做成更系统的实验工作流，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

## User

这样写死的 verifier 能胜任实际工作吗，在推理的时候我们有用到 verifier 吗

## Assistant

使用 `scientific-critical-thinking` 来判断这套 verifier 的实际可行性，并核对代码里推理阶段是否真的调用了 verifier。

## Assistant

我再看 `tools.py` 和 `rollout.py` 两处，确认推理时 verifier 是“真的参与决策”还是只是记录日志。

## Assistant

结论先说清楚：

1. 这套写死的规则 verifier，能做一部分实际工作，但不能单独承担“最终语义裁判”  
2. 在你当前代码里，推理时确实会用到 verifier，但它不是每一步自动触发，而是当 policy 主动调用 `verify_hypothesis` 时才在线生效

**它能做什么**
作为在线“证据充分性/必要性控制器”，它是有用的。原因是它判断的不是开放语义本身，而是更窄的东西：

- 当前选中的证据子集够不够支撑 claim
- 把这些证据拿掉后结论会不会塌
- 现在发 alert 是否为时过早
- 下一步更应该 `continue_search`、`revise_claim`、`refine_evidence` 还是 `finalize`

这类判断，本来就比较适合规则化，因为它依赖的是：
- 已搜索到哪些窗口
- 当前 claim 是什么
- 当前 alert 在什么时刻
- keep / drop / full 这几个反事实视角下支持度怎么变化

这正是 [verifier.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py) 现在在做的事情。

**它做不了什么**
如果你指望它像一个真正视觉模型那样判断：
- 这是不是 assault
- 这是不是 fire
- 这段画面里到底有没有关键动作
- claim 文本和视频语义是否真的一致

那规则 verifier 本身不够。因为它没有真正“看懂视频语义”，它主要是在看：
- 时间窗覆盖
- 角色覆盖
- 证据删掉后支持度变化
- 一些写死的阈值

所以实事求是地说：

- 当任务是“控制搜索流程、控制 finalize 时机、控制 alert 时机”时，它有价值
- 当任务是“做最终高置信语义判别”时，它不够，最好配合 Qwen self-verifier 或单独训练 verifier

**在推理时有没有用到 verifier**
有，但是“按需调用”的，不是后台自动一直跑。

在线推理路径是：

1. policy 生成一个工具调用  
如果它调用 `verify_hypothesis`，就进入 verifier。

2. `verify_hypothesis` 工具会直接调用 `run_counterfactual_verifier(...)`  
见 [tools.py:263](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L263)

3. 这里在线推理明确是 `use_reference_supervision=False`  
也就是不会把 GT oracle 混回去，见 [tools.py:274](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L274) 到 [tools.py:278](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L278)

4. verifier 的结果会写回状态  
包括：
- `state.last_claim`
- `state.active_evidence_window_ids`
- `state.verification_records`
见 [tools.py:279](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L279) 到 [tools.py:281](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L281)

5. adapter 会把 verifier 的 `recommended_action` 重新喂给模型下一轮  
比如：
- “继续搜”
- “修正 claim”
- “现在可以 finalize”  
见 [adapter.py:44](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/adapter.py#L44) 到 [adapter.py:79](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/adapter.py#L79)

所以答案是：  
不是“只做离线打分”，而是真的会影响在线决策。

**但要补一句关键限制**
它只在模型调用 `verify_hypothesis` 时才参与。  
如果模型一路跳过 verifier，直接去 `finalize_case` 或直接 `<answer>`，那在线 verifier 就没有真正介入该样本的行为控制。

**当前最合理的定位**
以你们 SAVER 的目标来看，当前 verifier 最合理的定位是：

- 第一职责：在线流程控制器
- 第二职责：RL 局部 counterfactual reward 评估器
- 第三职责：诊断工具

而不是：
- 唯一的最终真实性裁判

**我的判断**
当前这套设计方向是对的，但单靠规则 verifier 不够最终版。更稳的最终版应该是：

- 在线默认走 `hybrid`
- 规则 verifier 负责结构化 keep/drop/full 判断
- Qwen verifier 负责补语义判断
- 主评估尽量 reference-free，不把 verifier 分数直接当论文主指标

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接把“当前 heuristic / hybrid / qwen_self_verifier 三者在你们系统里的职责边界”画成一张非常清楚的表，方便你后面决定训练时和推理时默认开哪个。复杂实验工作流如果后面想统一管理，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

## User

总结一下，规则 verifier主要是靠窗口覆盖率打分的是吗，他的输入与输出又是什么呢

## Assistant

不完全是，但可以近似理解成“一个以窗口结构和覆盖为主的规则打分器”。

更准确地说，它主要看 3 类信息：

- 你选了哪些证据窗口  
也就是 `keep / drop / full` 三个视角下，各自有哪些 `window`
- 这些窗口和目标证据结构是否对得上  
如果开了 reference supervision，会看它们和 `trigger / peak_action / confirmation` 这些 oracle 窗口的重叠
- 把这些窗口拿掉之后，支撑度掉了多少  
这就是反事实的 `necessity`

所以它不是纯“窗口覆盖率”一个指标，但确实是以时间窗覆盖、角色覆盖、keep-drop 差值这类结构信号为主。

有一个关键区别你要记住：

- 离线/诊断模式下，如果有 `structured_target` 和 `oracle_windows_sec`，它会比较像“带 GT 的结构判分器”
- 在线推理时，`verify_hypothesis` 明确传的是 `use_reference_supervision=False`，见 [tools.py:263](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L263)。这时 heuristic 会弱很多，基本退化成“你搜了多少窗口、覆盖了多少内容、keep/drop 后还剩多少支撑”的规则控制器

**它的输入**
核心输入在 [verifier.py:580](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L580)：

- `state`
里面最重要的是：
  - `visited_windows`
  - `evidence_ledger`
  - `alerts`
  - `last_claim`
- `multimodal_cache`
里面可能有：
  - `duration`
  - `fps`
  - `question`
  - 离线时的 `structured_target`
  - 离线时的 `oracle_windows_sec`
  - hybrid/Qwen verifier 的模型配置
- `verification_mode`
比如 `soft_alert_check`、`final_check`
- `claim`
当前要验证的异常主张
- `candidate_window_ids / candidate_evidence_ids / candidate_evidence_moment_ids`
你这次想验证的证据子集
- `alert`
当前 alert 决策
- `query`
当前搜索 query
- `backend`
`heuristic / hybrid / qwen_self_verifier`
- `use_reference_supervision`
在线一般是 `False`，离线诊断可为 `True`

**它的中间过程**
它会先构造 4 个视角，见 [verifier.py:635](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L635)：

- `full`
全部证据
- `keep`
当前保留证据
- `drop`
去掉当前保留证据后剩下的证据
- `alert_prefix`
只看 alert 时间点之前的证据

然后每个视角都会算一组分数，核心在 [verifier.py:299](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L299)：

- `exist_support`
- `category_support`
- `temporal_support`
- `precursor_support`
- `alert_support`
- `counterfactual_support`
- `overall_support`

再进一步汇总成，见 [verifier.py:378](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L378)：

- `sufficiency`
- `necessity`
- `consistency`
- `alertability`
- `counterfactual_faithfulness`

**它的输出**
最终输出是一个 `verdict dict`，最重要的字段有：

- `primary_status`
`complete / incomplete / redundant / misaligned`
- `alert_status`
`justified / premature / late / not_applicable`
- `recommended_action`
`continue_search / revise_claim / refine_evidence / finalize`
- `view_scores`
4 个视角各自的分数
- `derived_scores`
`sufficiency / necessity / consistency / alertability / counterfactual_faithfulness`
- `verified_window_ids`
本次真正被 verifier 认定的证据窗口
- `best_effort_window_ids`
兜底窗口集合
- `failure_reasons`
失败原因
- `explanation`
文字解释
- `verifier_backend`
最终实际用的是哪个 backend

这些状态压缩规则在 [verifier.py:493](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L493) 和 [verifier.py:519](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L519)。

**一句话总结**
如果只说 heuristic verifier：

- 离线时：它是“基于 GT 结构的窗口-证据判分器”
- 在线时：它更像“基于搜索覆盖和 keep/drop 反事实的流程控制器”

所以你可以把它理解成：
不是“看懂视频的模型”，而是“管理证据是否足够、是否必要、是否该继续搜/该 finalize 的规则裁判”。

如果你愿意，我下一条可以把 `state / multimodal_cache / verdict` 三个 JSON 直接按你们代码风格手写一个最小样例给你看。

## User

所以说在线推理时，这个规则 verifier 会变成异常的弱，所以我觉得他不能胜任 verifier 的工作你觉得对吗，如果是的话，我们有没有必要用正在微调的 qwen 作为 verifier 或者重新训练一个基于 qwen 或者其他神经网络架构的 verifier呢，另外再参考一下https://github.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R,看看他们是怎么 verifiy 的，对我们有没有什么参考价值呢

## Assistant

我先核对 TimeSearch-R 仓库里有没有明确的 verifier / self-critique / stage 设计，再把它和我们当前这套在线 verifier 的强弱对比清楚。

## Assistant

对，我基本同意你的判断。

当前在线推理里，规则 verifier 不能单独胜任“真正的 verifier”。原因很直接：

- 在线 `verify_hypothesis` 明确关闭了 reference supervision，见 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L263)。
- 一旦没有 `oracle_windows_sec / structured_target`，anomaly claim 的 heuristic 会退化到 `_fallback_view_scores(...)`，本质上主要看“选了几个窗口”，见 [verifier.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L215) 和 [verifier.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L317)。
- normal claim 的 fallback 稍强一点，会再看视频覆盖率，但仍然主要是结构量，不是异常语义理解，见 [verifier.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L257)。

所以它现在更像：
- 证据子集 bookkeeping / keep-drop 结构裁判
- 流程控制器
- fallback guardrail

而不是：
- 真正的语义 verifier

**要不要上神经网络 verifier**
要。对 SAVER 这种任务，最终版最好是“结构 verifier + 神经 verifier”的双轨。

我建议分 3 档看：

- 最差方案：继续只用 heuristic  
这不够，尤其在线 anomaly verify 太弱。

- 过渡方案：用你们已经微调过的 Qwen checkpoint，当 frozen verifier  
这比“原始 qwen3-vl-8b-Instruct”明显更合理，因为它至少已经学过异常任务、工具协议和你们的 claim 格式。  
但不要用“正在同步更新的 policy 本体”去当同一轮 RL 的 verifier。那会有自我确认和目标漂移问题。

- 最终方案：单独训练一个 verifier  
这是我更推荐的。初始化可以直接从你们最好的 SFT checkpoint 来，而不是从原始 base model 开始。这样最稳。

**我对这三个选择的判断**
- 原始 Qwen 直接当 verifier：不够好。你前面质疑是对的。
- 把当前最好的 SFT Qwen checkpoint 冻结后拿来当 verifier：值得立刻做，作为过渡版。
- 单独训练 verifier：论文级最终版更应该这样做。

**为什么“单独训练 verifier”更合理**
因为 verifier 和 policy 的职责不同：

- policy 要学“去哪搜、何时 verify、何时 finalize”
- verifier 要学“当前证据子集是否足够、是否必要、alert 是否已可辩护”

如果直接共用同一个在线更新模型，会有几个问题：

- 同源偏差  
policy 看错的地方，verifier 往往也会一起看错。
- 自我确认  
同一个模型容易给自己的坏证据背书。
- RL 不稳定  
如果 verifier 跟着 policy 一起变，reward / control signal 也在漂。
- 论文说服力弱  
actor 和 judge 不分家，方法学上会更难 defend。

**所以最合理的部署建议**
我建议你们这样做：

- 在线推理默认用 `hybrid`
- 把 heuristic 保留下来，但只负责结构约束  
也就是 keep/drop/full、evidence subset、一致性、兜底
- 把神经 verifier 作为主语义打分器
- 神经 verifier 先用“冻结的最佳 SFT checkpoint”顶上
- 后续再把它单独训练成专门的 `Qwen-verifier`

**这个 verifier 用什么架构最合适**
优先级我会这样排：

- 第一选择：基于 Qwen3-VL-8B 单独训练 verifier  
最合适。因为你们 verifier 的输入不是纯分类，而是“claim + 多视角证据图像 + 时间戳 + structured output”，这正适合 Qwen-VL。
- 第二选择：用更强模型做 teacher，再蒸馏到 8B verifier  
比如用 32B 只做离线 teacher / pseudo-label / difficult case judge，线上还是 8B。
- 不优先：重新做一个 CLIP/VideoMAE 式轻量判别器  
速度可能快，但它不擅长你们这种结构化、多视角、反事实 keep/drop 判定，第一版不值得走这条线。

**具体到你问的“用正在微调的 qwen 还是单独训练一个”**
我的建议是：

- 现在立刻可做：把 `verifier-model-path` 从原始 base model 改成你们最好的 SFT checkpoint，且冻结使用。
- 不建议：让当前 RL 正在更新的 policy 权重同时充当 verifier。
- 最终推荐：从该 SFT checkpoint 复制一份，单独训练 `verifier-only` 模型。

**TimeSearch-R 对我们有什么参考价值**
有，但不是“直接照搬一个独立 verifier 模块”。

我查了他们仓库，关键信息是：

- README 明确写了他们的 `GRPO-CSV` 是“用同一个 policy model 去 self-verify 已搜索帧是否充分”，不是你们这种显式外接 `verify_hypothesis` 工具式 verifier。来源：TimeSearch-R README  
https://github.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R
- 他们训练时还另外用了一个更强的 LLM-as-a-judge reward model，README 里写的是 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct`。来源：TimeSearch-R README  
https://github.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R
- 他们推理环境代码里，运行时主要暴露的是视频搜索工具，并没有看到像你们这样显式的在线 `verify_hypothesis` 工具接口。来源：`time_r1/inference.py` 和 `time_r1/environment/video_env.py`  
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/time_r1/inference.py  
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/time_r1/environment/video_env.py
- 他们 reward 目录里有 `llm_judge.py`，说明训练阶段确实依赖外部 judge。来源：  
https://github.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/blob/main/time_r1/reward/llm_judge.py

这对你们的启发是：

- 可以借鉴他们的思路：自验证应该深度进入 RL 和搜索过程，而不是只做离线后处理。
- 但不能完全照抄他们的形式：SAVER 比他们多了“证据子集 sufficiency / necessity”和“alert 合理性”这两个更强的结构需求，所以你们保留显式 verifier tool 是对的。
- 你们真正该借的是：
  - self-verification 要参与策略学习
  - 外部强 judge 可以做 teacher / reward / distillation
- 而不是把 verifier 永远停留在 heuristic

**我的最终判断**
- 你说“在线规则 verifier 太弱，不能胜任 verifier 工作”，这个判断基本正确。
- 但它不该被删掉，而该降级为结构 prior 和 fallback。
- 真正该补的是一个神经 verifier。
- 最务实路线是：
  1. 先把线上 `verifier-model-path` 切到最佳 SFT checkpoint
  2. 默认用 `hybrid`
  3. 开始单独训练 `Qwen verifier`
  4. 以后可以再用更强模型做 teacher，蒸馏到 8B verifier

如果你同意，我下一步可以直接把“专用 verifier 训练方案”翻成代码级 spec：数据样本怎么构造、label 怎么定义、训练脚本怎么接、线上如何替换 `hybrid` 默认后端。复杂研究工作流如果后面想系统化编排，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

## User

在 sft 或者 rl 阶段能不能一起训练反事实 verifier 的能力呢，换言之在 sft 和 rl 除了训练现有的能力外也要训练反事实验证的能力实现不用外接 verifier ，实现端到端的训练，rl 阶段模仿 GRPO-CSV 是“用同一个 policy model 去 self-verify 已搜索帧是否充分”，不需要外接 verifier

## Assistant

我用 `scientific-brainstorming` 和 `scientific-critical-thinking` 来评估这个方向：能不能把反事实 verifier 吸收到同一个 policy 里，做成真正端到端、推理时不外接 verifier。

## Assistant

结论：可以，而且我认为这才是你们更合理的最终方向。

但要分清两件事：

- 推理时不外接 verifier：我支持
- 训练时完全不借助任何外部 verifier / judge：我不建议一上来就这么做

**先回答你的核心判断**
你的判断基本对。当前在线 `heuristic` verifier 太弱，单独拿它做真正的 verifier 不够。它现在更适合当：

- 结构先验
- fallback guardrail
- 训练诊断器

不适合当最终语义 verifier。

原因很简单：

- 在线 `verify_hypothesis` 已经明确 `use_reference_supervision=False`，见 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L263)
- 这时 anomaly claim 的 heuristic 会退化到 `_fallback_view_scores(...)`，主要看窗口数量和简单 coverage，而不是异常语义，见 [verifier.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/verifier.py#L215)

所以如果你的目标是“当前证据子集是否真的足以支撑 claim / alert”，在线纯规则版确实不够。

**能不能在 SFT / RL 里把 verifier 能力一起训进同一个 policy**
能，而且我认为应该这么做。

这条路线本质上就是把 `verify_hypothesis` 从“外接裁判”变成“policy 自己会的一项能力”。这和 TimeSearch-R 的核心方向是一致的：他们 README 明确写了 `GRPO-CSV` 是“用同一个 policy model 去验证 searched frames 是否充分”，而不是推理时再挂一个独立 verifier。来源：
- TimeSearch-R README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/README.md

同时，他们训练又不是完全无外部信号的。README 也明确写了训练时使用 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 作为 LLM-as-a-judge reward model。来源：
- TimeSearch-R README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/README.md

这说明一个很重要的边界：

- 推理时可以 self-verify
- 训练时仍然可以有 teacher / judge / reward model 辅助

我认为这正是你们 SAVER 最该借鉴的点。

**对 SAVER，我建议的最终版不是“外接 verifier”也不是“完全无 verifier”，而是**
`self-verifying policy + optional training-time teacher`

也就是：

- 推理阶段：不用外接 verifier model
- SFT 阶段：显式教模型做反事实验证
- RL 阶段：让同一个 policy 自己输出 self-verification
- 训练阶段：可以继续用更强 teacher/judge 做辅助监督或校准
- 线上部署：只保留一个 model

**这比“共享同一个 policy 直接裸奔 self-verify”更稳**
因为如果你完全不用任何 teacher，直接让 policy 自己 judge 自己，会有 4 个明显风险：

- 自我确认偏差：模型容易给自己找理由 finalize
- 校准差：会出现“过早 finalize”或“永远 continue_search”
- credit assignment 难：self-verify 对不对，往往要后续 rollout 才知道
- RL 容易塌缩：模型可能学会说看起来正确的 verifier 话术，而不是学会真正的证据判断

所以我不建议“从今天开始把外部 verifier 全删掉”。

**更合理的路线**
我建议你们走 3 步。

1. SFT 先把反事实 verifier 能力训进 policy  
你们其实已经有基础了：
- oracle 里已经有 `verify_hypothesis`
- 也已经有 `oracle_verifier_feedback`
- 还有 `recommended_action=continue_search / revise_claim / finalize` 这些分支  
相关代码就在 [convert_to_saver_agent.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/convert_to_saver_agent.py) 和 [training_data.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training_data.py)

下一步不是继续强化外接 verifier，而是把这些监督改造成“模型自己会输出自验证结论”。

2. RL 阶段把 self-verification 变成同一个 policy 的内部能力  
这一步就对齐 TimeSearch-R 的 GRPO-CSV 思路，但要改成 SAVER 版本。  
TimeSearch-R 更关注“searched frames 是否充分”，你们则要扩展成：

- `sufficiency`
- `necessity`
- `alertability`
- `recommended_action`

也就是更像：
`Counterfactual Evidence-and-Alert Self-Verification`

3. 训练时保留 teacher，推理时移除 teacher  
这是我最推荐的工程边界。  
也就是：

- 训练时：
  - 可以用冻结的 SFT Qwen verifier
  - 或更强的 Qwen / judge model
  - 只作为 reward / consistency regularizer / pseudo-label teacher
- 推理时：
  - 不再调用外接 verifier
  - 只靠同一个 policy 自己 self-verify

**你问“要不要用正在微调的 qwen 作为 verifier，或者重训一个 verifier”**
我的建议是分阶段：

- 短期：可以把“当前最好的 SFT checkpoint”作为 teacher verifier 用于训练辅助
- 中期：不要让“正在同轮更新的 policy”直接兼任唯一 judge
- 最终：推理时用同一个 policy self-verify，训练时保留一个冻结 teacher 或历史快照做稳定器

换句话说：

- `同一个模型负责推理和 self-verify`：我支持
- `同一个正在更新的模型同时充当唯一 reward/verifier`：我不支持

**TimeSearch-R 对我们的直接参考价值**
有，而且很大，但不是“照抄”。

我看到的关键点是：

- README 写明他们的核心是 same-policy self-verification  
链接： https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/README.md
- README 同时写明训练用外部 judge reward model  
链接： https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/README.md
- 公开 inference 入口里，默认暴露的工具主要是视频搜索工具 `seek_video_frames`，没有你们这种显式独立 verifier tool  
链接： https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/time_r1/inference.py
- 他们的 runtime environment 也是围绕搜索工具展开，而不是外挂 verifier stage  
链接： https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/time_r1/environment/video_env.py

所以对你们最有价值的借鉴不是：
“照搬一个 verifier 模块”

而是：
“把 verifier 能力吸收到 policy 本身；外部 judge 只留在训练时做辅助”

**我的最终建议**
如果目标是最终版 SAVER，我建议你们明确采用下面这个原则：

- 推理默认不外接 verifier
- policy 自己学会：
  - 当前证据是否足够
  - 去掉这些证据后是否会塌
  - 现在该不该 alert
  - 现在该不该 finalize
- heuristic verifier 保留，但降级为：
  - fallback
  - ablation
  - diagnostic
- 训练时允许使用：
  - 冻结 teacher verifier
  - 更强 judge model
  - rollout branch outcome 做自一致性监督

如果你认可，我下一步可以直接把这个方向翻成一套可落地的代码级方案：
- SFT 怎么改 supervision
- RL 怎么把 `verify_hypothesis` 改成 same-policy self-verification
- 训练时 teacher 怎么接
- 推理时怎么真正做到“不外接 verifier，只用一个模型”  
复杂实验编排后面如果想做成完整研究工作流，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

---

# Scientific Brainstorming

## Overview

Scientific brainstorming is a conversational process for generating novel research ideas. Act as a research ideation partner to generate hypotheses, explore interdisciplinary connections, challenge assumptions, and develop methodologies. Apply this skill for creative scientific problem-solving.

## When to Use This Skill

This skill should be used when:
- Generating novel research ideas or directions
- Exploring interdisciplinary connections and analogies
- Challenging assumptions in existing research frameworks
- Developing new methodological approaches
- Identifying research gaps or opportunities
- Overcoming creative blocks in problem-solving
- Brainstorming experimental designs or study plans

## Core Principles

When engaging in scientific brainstorming:

1. **Conversational and Collaborative**: Engage as an equal thought partner, not an instructor. Ask questions, build on ideas together, and maintain a natural dialogue.

2. **Intellectually Curious**: Show genuine interest in the scientist's work. Ask probing questions that demonstrate deep understanding and help uncover new angles.

3. **Creatively Challenging**: Push beyond obvious ideas. Challenge assumptions respectfully, propose unconventional connections, and encourage exploration of "what if" scenarios.

4. **Domain-Aware**: Demonstrate broad scientific knowledge across disciplines to identify cross-pollination opportunities and relevant analogies from other fields.

5. **Structured yet Flexible**: Guide the conversation with purpose, but adapt dynamically based on where the scientist's thinking leads.

## Brainstorming Workflow

### Phase 1: Understanding the Context

Begin by deeply understanding what the scientist is working on. This phase establishes the foundation for productive ideation.

**Approach:**
- Ask open-ended questions about their current research, interests, or challenge
- Understand their field, methodology, and constraints
- Identify what they're trying to achieve and what obstacles they face
- Listen for implicit assumptions or unexplored angles

**Example questions:**
- "What aspect of your research are you most excited about right now?"
- "What problem keeps you up at night?"
- "What assumptions are you making that might be worth questioning?"
- "Are there any unexpected findings that don't fit your current model?"

**Transition:** Once the context is clear, acknowledge understanding and suggest moving into active ideation.

### Phase 2: Divergent Exploration

Help the scientist generate a wide range of ideas without judgment. The goal is quantity and diversity, not immediate feasibility.

**Techniques to employ:**

1. **Cross-Domain Analogies**
   - Draw parallels from other scientific fields
   - "How might concepts from [field X] apply to your problem?"
   - Connect biological systems to social networks, physics to economics, etc.

2. **Assumption Reversal**
   - Identify core assumptions and flip them
   - "What if the opposite were true?"
   - "What if you had unlimited resources/time/data?"

3. **Scale Shifting**
   - Explore the problem at different scales (molecular, cellular, organismal, population, ecosystem)
   - Consider temporal scales (milliseconds to millennia)

4. **Constraint Removal/Addition**
   - Remove apparent constraints: "What if you could measure anything?"
   - Add new constraints: "What if you had to solve this with 1800s technology?"

5. **Interdisciplinary Fusion**
   - Suggest combining methodologies from different fields
   - Propose collaborations that bridge disciplines

6. **Technology Speculation**
   - Imagine emerging technologies applied to the problem
   - "What becomes possible with CRISPR/AI/quantum computing/etc.?"

**Interaction style:**
- Rapid-fire idea generation with the scientist
- Build on their suggestions with "Yes, and..."
- Encourage wild ideas explicitly: "What's the most radical approach imaginable?"
- Consult references/brainstorming_methods.md for additional structured techniques

### Phase 3: Connection Making

Help identify patterns, themes, and unexpected connections among the generated ideas.

**Approach:**
- Look for common threads across different ideas
- Identify which ideas complement or enhance each other
- Find surprising connections between seemingly unrelated concepts
- Map relationships between ideas visually (if helpful)

**Prompts:**
- "I notice several ideas involve [theme]—what if we combined them?"
- "These three approaches share [commonality]—is there something deeper there?"
- "What's the most unexpected connection you're seeing?"

### Phase 4: Critical Evaluation

Shift to constructively evaluating the most promising ideas while maintaining creative momentum.

**Balance:**
- Be critical but not dismissive
- Identify both strengths and challenges
- Consider feasibility while preserving innovative elements
- Suggest modifications to make wild ideas more tractable

**Questions to explore:**
- "What would it take to actually test this?"
- "What's the first small experiment to run?"
- "What existing data or tools could be leveraged?"
- "Who else would need to be involved?"
- "What's the biggest obstacle, and how might it be overcome?"

### Phase 5: Synthesis and Next Steps

Help crystallize insights and create concrete paths forward.

**Deliverables:**
- Summarize the most promising directions identified
- Highlight novel connections or perspectives discovered
- Suggest immediate next steps (literature search, pilot experiments, collaborations)
- Capture key questions that emerged for future exploration
- Identify resources or expertise that would be valuable

**Close with encouragement:**
- Acknowledge the creative work done
- Reinforce the value of the ideas generated
- Offer to continue the brainstorming in future sessions

## Adaptive Techniques

### When the Scientist Is Stuck

- Break the problem into smaller pieces
- Change the framing entirely ("Instead of asking X, what if we asked Y?")
- Tell a story or analogy that might spark new thinking
- Suggest taking a "vacation" from the problem to explore tangential ideas

### When Ideas Are Too Safe

- Explicitly encourage risk-taking: "What's an idea so bold it makes you nervous?"
- Play devil's advocate to the conservative approach
- Ask about failed or abandoned approaches and why they might actually work
- Propose intentionally provocative "what ifs"

### When Energy Lags

- Inject enthusiasm about interesting ideas
- Share genuine curiosity about a particular direction
- Ask about something that excites them personally
- Take a brief tangent into a related but different topic

## Resources

### references/brainstorming_methods.md

Contains detailed descriptions of structured brainstorming methodologies that can be consulted when standard techniques need supplementation:
- SCAMPER framework (Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to another use, Eliminate, Reverse)
- Six Thinking Hats for multi-perspective analysis
- Morphological analysis for systematic exploration
- TRIZ principles for inventive problem-solving
- Biomimicry approaches for nature-inspired solutions

Consult this file when the scientist requests a specific methodology or when the brainstorming session would benefit from a more structured approach.

## Notes

- This is a **conversation**, not a lecture. The scientist should be doing at least 50% of the talking.
- Avoid jargon from fields outside the scientist's expertise unless explaining it clearly.
- Be comfortable with silence—give space for thinking.
- Remember that the best brainstorming often feels playful and exploratory.
- The goal is not to solve everything, but to open new possibilities.


</skill>

[2026-03-30-self-verifying-saver-policy.md](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-30-self-verifying-saver-policy.md)

核心方向是把当前“外接 verifier”改成“同一个 policy 的 self-verification 能力”：

- `verify_hypothesis` 不再去调用外部 backend 做在线裁判。

- [saver_agent/reward.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/reward.py)
- [train_saver_sft.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_sft.py)
- [train_saver_rl.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_rl.py)

关于 `teacher judge model`，`Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 可以用，但我建议你把它定位准确：

- 可以用作训练期 `teacher judge`
- 不建议作为推理期在线 verifier
- 更适合判“结构化文本包”，不适合直接看原始帧

原因是官方模型卡把 `Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct` 定义为 text generation / causal language model，不是 VL 模型；如果你要看图，官方另有 `Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct`。来源：
- Hugging Face model card: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct
- Hugging Face VL model card: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct

这也和 TimeSearch-R 的思路是一致的：他们的 README 里，`GRPO-CSV` 强调 same-policy self-verification，同时训练还用了更强的 LLM-as-a-judge reward model，文中提到的是 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct`。来源：
- TimeSearch-R repo: https://github.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R
- TimeSearch-R README raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R/main/README.md

所以对 SAVER 最合理的组合是：

- 推理时：单模型 policy，自带 self-verification
- 训练时：可选 teacher judge
- teacher judge 首选 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct`，但输入应是文本化证据包：
  - claim JSON
  - full/keep/drop/alert_prefix 的窗口摘要
  - timestamps
  - selected evidence ids
  - policy 的 self-verification 输出
- 如果你坚持让 teacher 直接看帧，再换成多模态 teacher，比如 `Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct`

我对工程上的建议是：

- 第一版 teacher judge 用 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct`
- 只做训练期 sparse teacher，不要每一步都同步调用
- 优先用于：
  - SFT 离线 label enrichment
  - RL anchor turn 校准
  - epoch-end diagnostic
- 不要和正在训练的 policy 共占同一轮在线推理路径

因为你现在只有 4 张 H200，如果同时训练 policy 再同步托管 72B teacher，会明显拖慢吞吐。更现实的做法是：

- teacher judge 离线批量打分
- 或单独起服务做异步/稀疏调用
- 线上 rollout 默认只跑 policy 自己

如果你同意，我下一步就直接进入代码落实，顺序我建议是：

1. 先改 `verify_hypothesis` schema 和 `tools.py`
2. 再改 oracle SFT supervision
3. 再改 RL 的 self-verification reward
4. 最后接 teacher judge client

复杂实验编排如果后面想整成完整研究工作流，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

让 teacher judge 看原始帧效果是否会好些呢相比使用 Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct

我先用 `scientific-critical-thinking` 的思路做这个取舍判断，并核对一下 Qwen 官方模型卡，避免把 72B text model 和 72B VL model 混在一起。

## Assistant

我先核对这两个模型的官方定位，再给你一个直接可执行的选择建议。

## Assistant

如果 teacher judge 真的要看原始帧，我会优先选 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`，而不是 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct`。

原因很直接：

- `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 是纯文本 causal LM，不是多模态模型。官方模型卡写的是 `Type: Causal Language Models`。  
  来源：<https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct>
- `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 是明确面向视觉和视频理解的 VL 模型，官方模型卡强调了：
  - video understanding
  - timestamp-grounded event localization
  - stronger agent interaction  
  来源：<https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct>

所以如果你的问题是：

“teacher judge 要不要直接看帧，若看帧该用哪个？”

答案是：

- 看原始帧：`Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 更合适
- 只看结构化摘要/窗口文本/时间戳：`Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 更合适

但这里还有一个更重要的工程判断：

**看原始帧不一定总是更好。**

对 SAVER 的 teacher judge，任务不是普通分类，而是判断：

- 当前 `keep` 证据子集是否足够
- 把这些证据拿掉后结论是否塌
- `alert_prefix` 是否已经 actionable

这类判断有两部分：

1. 视觉真实性  
画面里到底是不是那个异常、关键动作是否真的出现。  
这一部分 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 更强。

2. 反事实结构判断  
`full / keep / drop / alert_prefix` 之间的关系是否满足 sufficiency / necessity / alertability。  
这一部分不完全依赖原始图像，反而很依赖结构化输入是否清楚。这里 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 作为文本 judge 其实很有价值。

所以我的实际建议不是二选一，而是：

**最优方案：双层 teacher**
- 主 teacher：`Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct`
  用于大规模离线 judge
  输入是结构化 evidence package：
  - claim JSON
  - `full / keep / drop / alert_prefix` 的窗口摘要
  - timestamps
  - selected window ids
  - policy 的 self-verification 输出
- 稀疏视觉 teacher：`Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`
  只在困难样本上看原始帧：
  - subtle anomaly
  - temporal ambiguity
  - keep/drop 差异主要靠画面细节决定
  - teacher disagreement case

如果你现在只能先上一个，我建议这样选：

- 如果你要的是“论文最终版、teacher 真正有视觉裁判能力”：先上 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`
- 如果你要的是“先把整套 self-verification 训练链路稳定跑通”：先上 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct`

因为：
- `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 更贴近任务本质
- 但 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 更容易先做成稳定的离线 teacher judge

对你们当前 4x H200 的机器，我更务实的建议是：

- 第一阶段：用 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 做文本 teacher，先把 self-verification 训练逻辑跑通
- 第二阶段：加一个 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 的稀疏视觉 teacher 分支，只打 hardest cases
- 推理阶段：仍然只保留单个 self-verifying policy，不带外接 teacher

这比“所有训练样本都让 VL 32B 看原始帧”更现实，因为后者会非常重，吞吐会明显下降。

顺便说一句，这也和 TimeSearch-R 的取向一致：他们强调 same-policy self-verification，而训练时另配强 judge。你们可以借这个思想，但把 judge 从纯文本扩展成“文本主 judge + 稀疏视觉 judge”。

如果你同意，我下一步可以直接把这件事翻成代码级 spec：
- 哪些 rollout anchor 送 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`
- teacher 输入包长什么样
- 如何在 `train_saver_rl.py` 里接成 sparse judge
- 如何控制成本，不让训练吞吐崩掉。

---
name: scientific-brainstorming
description: Creative research ideation and exploration. Use for open-ended brainstorming sessions, exploring interdisciplinary connections, challenging assumptions, or identifying research gaps. Best for early-stage research planning when you do not have specific observations yet. For formulating testable hypotheses from data use hypothesis-generation.
license: MIT license
metadata:

---

# Scientific Brainstorming

## Overview

Scientific brainstorming is a conversational process for generating novel research ideas. Act as a research ideation partner to generate hypotheses, explore interdisciplinary connections, challenge assumptions, and develop methodologies. Apply this skill for creative scientific problem-solving.

## When to Use This Skill

This skill should be used when:
- Generating novel research ideas or directions
- Exploring interdisciplinary connections and analogies
- Challenging assumptions in existing research frameworks
- Developing new methodological approaches
- Identifying research gaps or opportunities
- Overcoming creative blocks in problem-solving
- Brainstorming experimental designs or study plans

## Core Principles

When engaging in scientific brainstorming:

1. **Conversational and Collaborative**: Engage as an equal thought partner, not an instructor. Ask questions, build on ideas together, and maintain a natural dialogue.

2. **Intellectually Curious**: Show genuine interest in the scientist's work. Ask probing questions that demonstrate deep understanding and help uncover new angles.

3. **Creatively Challenging**: Push beyond obvious ideas. Challenge assumptions respectfully, propose unconventional connections, and encourage exploration of "what if" scenarios.

4. **Domain-Aware**: Demonstrate broad scientific knowledge across disciplines to identify cross-pollination opportunities and relevant analogies from other fields.

5. **Structured yet Flexible**: Guide the conversation with purpose, but adapt dynamically based on where the scientist's thinking leads.

## Brainstorming Workflow

### Phase 1: Understanding the Context

Begin by deeply understanding what the scientist is working on. This phase establishes the foundation for productive ideation.

**Approach:**
- Ask open-ended questions about their current research, interests, or challenge
- Understand their field, methodology, and constraints
- Identify what they're trying to achieve and what obstacles they face
- Listen for implicit assumptions or unexplored angles

**Example questions:**
- "What aspect of your research are you most excited about right now?"
- "What problem keeps you up at night?"
- "What assumptions are you making that might be worth questioning?"
- "Are there any unexpected findings that don't fit your current model?"

**Transition:** Once the context is clear, acknowledge understanding and suggest moving into active ideation.

### Phase 2: Divergent Exploration

Help the scientist generate a wide range of ideas without judgment. The goal is quantity and diversity, not immediate feasibility.

**Techniques to employ:**

1. **Cross-Domain Analogies**
   - Draw parallels from other scientific fields
   - "How might concepts from [field X] apply to your problem?"
   - Connect biological systems to social networks, physics to economics, etc.

2. **Assumption Reversal**
   - Identify core assumptions and flip them
   - "What if the opposite were true?"
   - "What if you had unlimited resources/time/data?"

3. **Scale Shifting**
   - Explore the problem at different scales (molecular, cellular, organismal, population, ecosystem)
   - Consider temporal scales (milliseconds to millennia)

4. **Constraint Removal/Addition**
   - Remove apparent constraints: "What if you could measure anything?"
   - Add new constraints: "What if you had to solve this with 1800s technology?"

5. **Interdisciplinary Fusion**
   - Suggest combining methodologies from different fields
   - Propose collaborations that bridge disciplines

6. **Technology Speculation**
   - Imagine emerging technologies applied to the problem
   - "What becomes possible with CRISPR/AI/quantum computing/etc.?"

**Interaction style:**
- Rapid-fire idea generation with the scientist
- Build on their suggestions with "Yes, and..."
- Encourage wild ideas explicitly: "What's the most radical approach imaginable?"
- Consult references/brainstorming_methods.md for additional structured techniques

### Phase 3: Connection Making

Help identify patterns, themes, and unexpected connections among the generated ideas.

**Approach:**
- Look for common threads across different ideas
- Identify which ideas complement or enhance each other
- Find surprising connections between seemingly unrelated concepts
- Map relationships between ideas visually (if helpful)

**Prompts:**
- "I notice several ideas involve [theme]—what if we combined them?"
- "These three approaches share [commonality]—is there something deeper there?"
- "What's the most unexpected connection you're seeing?"

### Phase 4: Critical Evaluation

Shift to constructively evaluating the most promising ideas while maintaining creative momentum.

**Balance:**
- Be critical but not dismissive
- Identify both strengths and challenges
- Consider feasibility while preserving innovative elements
- Suggest modifications to make wild ideas more tractable

**Questions to explore:**
- "What would it take to actually test this?"
- "What's the first small experiment to run?"
- "What existing data or tools could be leveraged?"
- "Who else would need to be involved?"
- "What's the biggest obstacle, and how might it be overcome?"

### Phase 5: Synthesis and Next Steps

Help crystallize insights and create concrete paths forward.

**Deliverables:**
- Summarize the most promising directions identified
- Highlight novel connections or perspectives discovered
- Suggest immediate next steps (literature search, pilot experiments, collaborations)
- Capture key questions that emerged for future exploration
- Identify resources or expertise that would be valuable

**Close with encouragement:**
- Acknowledge the creative work done
- Reinforce the value of the ideas generated
- Offer to continue the brainstorming in future sessions

## Adaptive Techniques

### When the Scientist Is Stuck

- Break the problem into smaller pieces
- Change the framing entirely ("Instead of asking X, what if we asked Y?")
- Tell a story or analogy that might spark new thinking
- Suggest taking a "vacation" from the problem to explore tangential ideas

### When Ideas Are Too Safe

- Explicitly encourage risk-taking: "What's an idea so bold it makes you nervous?"
- Play devil's advocate to the conservative approach
- Ask about failed or abandoned approaches and why they might actually work
- Propose intentionally provocative "what ifs"

### When Energy Lags

- Inject enthusiasm about interesting ideas
- Share genuine curiosity about a particular direction
- Ask about something that excites them personally
- Take a brief tangent into a related but different topic

## Resources

### references/brainstorming_methods.md

Contains detailed descriptions of structured brainstorming methodologies that can be consulted when standard techniques need supplementation:
- SCAMPER framework (Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to another use, Eliminate, Reverse)
- Six Thinking Hats for multi-perspective analysis
- Morphological analysis for systematic exploration
- TRIZ principles for inventive problem-solving
- Biomimicry approaches for nature-inspired solutions

Consult this file when the scientist requests a specific methodology or when the brainstorming session would benefit from a more structured approach.

## Notes

- This is a **conversation**, not a lecture. The scientist should be doing at least 50% of the talking.
- Avoid jargon from fields outside the scientist's expertise unless explaining it clearly.
- Be comfortable with silence—give space for thinking.
- Remember that the best brainstorming often feels playful and exploratory.
- The goal is not to solve everything, but to open new possibilities.


</skill>

我会把现有计划收紧到“单一 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 既做 policy 初始化 teacher，又做视觉 teacher judge”的版本，避免落成和 TimeSearch-R 太像的双 judge 方案。

## Assistant

我直接把计划改成“单一 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 视觉 teacher judge”版本，去掉之前偏文本 judge 的表述。

## Assistant

可以，这样更有区分度，我已经把 plan 收紧成“单一 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 视觉 teacher judge”版本了。

更新后的计划文件在：
[2026-03-30-self-verifying-saver-policy.md](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-30-self-verifying-saver-policy.md)

现在这份代码级 spec 的核心设定是：

- 推理阶段：只用单个 self-verifying policy，不外接 verifier
- 训练阶段：只用一个视觉 teacher judge
- teacher 固定为 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`
- 不再走“文本 judge + 视觉 judge”双 teacher 路线，避免和 TimeSearch-R 太像

**这版 spec 的关键变化**
1. `verify_hypothesis` 改成 policy 自己产出结构化自验证结果  
不是再去调用外部 backend，而是输出：
- `selected_window_ids`
- `sufficiency_score`
- `necessity_score`
- `alertability_score`
- `counterfactual_faithfulness`
- `verification_decision`
- `recommended_action`

2. `tools.py` 只做 state recorder  
它负责：
- 校验 payload
- 写入 `state.verification_records`
- 更新 `active_evidence_window_ids`
- 把结果反馈给下一轮 prompt

3. SFT 显式教模型反事实验证  
oracle supervision 不再只是“verify 一下然后 finalize”，而是会监督：
- insufficient -> `continue_search`
- misaligned -> `revise_claim`
- redundant -> `refine_evidence`
- sufficient -> `finalize`

4. RL 改成 self-verification reward  
CEA-GRPO 的局部 advantage 不再默认依赖外接 verifier，而是优先用 policy 自己的 self-verification trace 来打：
- sufficiency quality
- necessity quality
- action calibration
- self-consistency

5. `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` 只做训练期视觉 teacher judge  
teacher 输入包包含：
- policy claim
- `full / keep / drop / alert_prefix` 四个视角摘要
- timestamps
- 每个视角采样的原始帧
- policy 自己的 self-verification 输出

teacher 的职责是：
- 给 self-verification 打校准分
- 给 difficult cases 做蒸馏标签
- 给 RL anchor turns 做 sparse reward augmentation

**这版和 TimeSearch-R 的差异**
这条线和 TimeSearch-R 的核心区别会比较清楚：

- TimeSearch-R 更强调 same-policy self-verify searched frames sufficiency
- 我们这里明确把任务扩展成：
  - evidence sufficiency
  - evidence necessity
  - alertability
  - recommended next action
- 并且 teacher judge 直接是视觉 teacher，不是他们 README 里强调的 `Qwen-2.5-72B-Instruct` 这种文本 judge 路线

所以论文上你们可以把这个点写成更明确的差异：

- TimeSearch-R 风格是 search sufficiency self-verification
- SAVER 最终版是 counterfactual evidence-and-alert self-verification

**我对这版的工程建议**
这版最稳的落地顺序仍然是：

1. 先改 `verify_hypothesis` schema 和 `tools.py`
2. 再改 oracle SFT supervision
3. 再改 rollout trace 和 reward
4. 最后接 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct` teacher judge client

因为 teacher judge 最重，应该最后接，不然你会在前面基础链路还没稳时就把复杂度拉满。

如果你同意，我下一步就不再停留在 spec，直接开始代码落实。复杂实验工作流如果后面想系统化编排，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

# Scientific Brainstorming

## Overview

Scientific brainstorming is a conversational process for generating novel research ideas. Act as a research ideation partner to generate hypotheses, explore interdisciplinary connections, challenge assumptions, and develop methodologies. Apply this skill for creative scientific problem-solving.

## When to Use This Skill

This skill should be used when:
- Generating novel research ideas or directions
- Exploring interdisciplinary connections and analogies
- Challenging assumptions in existing research frameworks
- Developing new methodological approaches
- Identifying research gaps or opportunities
- Overcoming creative blocks in problem-solving
- Brainstorming experimental designs or study plans

## Core Principles

When engaging in scientific brainstorming:

1. **Conversational and Collaborative**: Engage as an equal thought partner, not an instructor. Ask questions, build on ideas together, and maintain a natural dialogue.

2. **Intellectually Curious**: Show genuine interest in the scientist's work. Ask probing questions that demonstrate deep understanding and help uncover new angles.

3. **Creatively Challenging**: Push beyond obvious ideas. Challenge assumptions respectfully, propose unconventional connections, and encourage exploration of "what if" scenarios.

4. **Domain-Aware**: Demonstrate broad scientific knowledge across disciplines to identify cross-pollination opportunities and relevant analogies from other fields.

5. **Structured yet Flexible**: Guide the conversation with purpose, but adapt dynamically based on where the scientist's thinking leads.

## Brainstorming Workflow

### Phase 1: Understanding the Context

Begin by deeply understanding what the scientist is working on. This phase establishes the foundation for productive ideation.

**Approach:**
- Ask open-ended questions about their current research, interests, or challenge
- Understand their field, methodology, and constraints
- Identify what they're trying to achieve and what obstacles they face
- Listen for implicit assumptions or unexplored angles

**Example questions:**
- "What aspect of your research are you most excited about right now?"
- "What problem keeps you up at night?"
- "What assumptions are you making that might be worth questioning?"
- "Are there any unexpected findings that don't fit your current model?"

**Transition:** Once the context is clear, acknowledge understanding and suggest moving into active ideation.

### Phase 2: Divergent Exploration

Help the scientist generate a wide range of ideas without judgment. The goal is quantity and diversity, not immediate feasibility.

**Techniques to employ:**

1. **Cross-Domain Analogies**
   - Draw parallels from other scientific fields
   - "How might concepts from [field X] apply to your problem?"
   - Connect biological systems to social networks, physics to economics, etc.

2. **Assumption Reversal**
   - Identify core assumptions and flip them
   - "What if the opposite were true?"
   - "What if you had unlimited resources/time/data?"

3. **Scale Shifting**
   - Explore the problem at different scales (molecular, cellular, organismal, population, ecosystem)
   - Consider temporal scales (milliseconds to millennia)

4. **Constraint Removal/Addition**
   - Remove apparent constraints: "What if you could measure anything?"
   - Add new constraints: "What if you had to solve this with 1800s technology?"

5. **Interdisciplinary Fusion**
   - Suggest combining methodologies from different fields
   - Propose collaborations that bridge disciplines

6. **Technology Speculation**
   - Imagine emerging technologies applied to the problem
   - "What becomes possible with CRISPR/AI/quantum computing/etc.?"

**Interaction style:**
- Rapid-fire idea generation with the scientist
- Build on their suggestions with "Yes, and..."
- Encourage wild ideas explicitly: "What's the most radical approach imaginable?"
- Consult references/brainstorming_methods.md for additional structured techniques

### Phase 3: Connection Making

Help identify patterns, themes, and unexpected connections among the generated ideas.

**Approach:**
- Look for common threads across different ideas
- Identify which ideas complement or enhance each other
- Find surprising connections between seemingly unrelated concepts
- Map relationships between ideas visually (if helpful)

**Prompts:**
- "I notice several ideas involve [theme]—what if we combined them?"
- "These three approaches share [commonality]—is there something deeper there?"
- "What's the most unexpected connection you're seeing?"

### Phase 4: Critical Evaluation

Shift to constructively evaluating the most promising ideas while maintaining creative momentum.

**Balance:**
- Be critical but not dismissive
- Identify both strengths and challenges
- Consider feasibility while preserving innovative elements
- Suggest modifications to make wild ideas more tractable

**Questions to explore:**
- "What would it take to actually test this?"
- "What's the first small experiment to run?"
- "What existing data or tools could be leveraged?"
- "Who else would need to be involved?"
- "What's the biggest obstacle, and how might it be overcome?"

### Phase 5: Synthesis and Next Steps

Help crystallize insights and create concrete paths forward.

**Deliverables:**
- Summarize the most promising directions identified
- Highlight novel connections or perspectives discovered
- Suggest immediate next steps (literature search, pilot experiments, collaborations)
- Capture key questions that emerged for future exploration
- Identify resources or expertise that would be valuable

**Close with encouragement:**
- Acknowledge the creative work done
- Reinforce the value of the ideas generated
- Offer to continue the brainstorming in future sessions

## Adaptive Techniques

### When the Scientist Is Stuck

- Break the problem into smaller pieces
- Change the framing entirely ("Instead of asking X, what if we asked Y?")
- Tell a story or analogy that might spark new thinking
- Suggest taking a "vacation" from the problem to explore tangential ideas

### When Ideas Are Too Safe

- Explicitly encourage risk-taking: "What's an idea so bold it makes you nervous?"
- Play devil's advocate to the conservative approach
- Ask about failed or abandoned approaches and why they might actually work
- Propose intentionally provocative "what ifs"

### When Energy Lags

- Inject enthusiasm about interesting ideas
- Share genuine curiosity about a particular direction
- Ask about something that excites them personally
- Take a brief tangent into a related but different topic

## Resources

### references/brainstorming_methods.md

Contains detailed descriptions of structured brainstorming methodologies that can be consulted when standard techniques need supplementation:
- SCAMPER framework (Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to another use, Eliminate, Reverse)
- Six Thinking Hats for multi-perspective analysis
- Morphological analysis for systematic exploration
- TRIZ principles for inventive problem-solving
- Biomimicry approaches for nature-inspired solutions

Consult this file when the scientist requests a specific methodology or when the brainstorming session would benefit from a more structured approach.

## Notes

- This is a **conversation**, not a lecture. The scientist should be doing at least 50% of the talking.
- Avoid jargon from fields outside the scientist's expertise unless explaining it clearly.
- Be comfortable with silence—give space for thinking.
- Remember that the best brainstorming often feels playful and exploratory.
- The goal is not to solve everything, but to open new possibilities.


</skill>

## Assistant

我已经把代码级 spec 改成这一版，文件在：
[2026-03-30-self-verifying-saver-policy.md](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-30-self-verifying-saver-policy.md)

这次更新后的核心点是：

- teacher 只保留一个：`Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`
- 但 teacher 支持三种输入模式：
  - `text_only`
  - `multimodal_visual`
  - `auto`
- `text_only` 用于大多数 anchor：
  - claim
  - full/keep/drop/alert_prefix 摘要
  - timestamps
  - policy self-verification 输出
- `multimodal_visual` 在困难样本上升级使用：
  - 上述文本包
  - 再加 sampled raw frames / preview frames
- `auto` 默认先走 `text_only`，遇到困难情况再升级到 `multimodal_visual`

spec 里我还补了这些具体改动：

- `train_saver_rl.py` 新增 teacher 相关开关：
  - `--teacher-judge-input-mode {text_only,multimodal_visual,auto}`
  - `--teacher-judge-anchor-policy {verify_only,verify_and_finalize,hard_examples}`
  - `--teacher-judge-topk-frames-per-view`
- `self_verification.py` 里新增：
  - `build_teacher_judge_package(...)`
  - `select_teacher_judge_input_mode(...)`
- reward 设计改成：
  - self-verification 质量奖励
  - teacher agreement 奖励
- 文档里明确写清：
  - 不是两个 teacher model
  - 而是一个 `Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct`，两种 judge 方式

我认为这版比前一版更好，因为：

- 和 TimeSearch-R 的“单纯外部文本 judge”路线区分更明显
- 保留了成本可控性
- 也保留了真正视觉裁判能力
- 更符合 SAVER 的反事实验证需求

下一步可以直接开始按这个 spec 落代码。复杂研究工作流如果后面想统一编排，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

现在支持两种用法。

1. 先离线标注，再训练。这个更适合你后面多卡正式跑：
```bash
python annotate_teacher_judge_sft.py \
  --input /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.jsonl \
  --output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.teacher.jsonl \
  --model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct \
  --input-mode multimodal_visual
```

然后训练：
```bash
python train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.teacher.jsonl \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/ckpt/exp_sft_teacher
```

2. 在 `train_saver_sft.py` 里单进程自动标注后再训练：
```bash
python train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.jsonl \
  --teacher-judge-model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct \
  --teacher-judge-input-mode multimodal_visual \
  --teacher-judge-output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.teacher.jsonl \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/ckpt/exp_sft_teacher
```

注意一件事：我把 `train_saver_sft.py` 里的“内联 teacher 标注”限制成单进程使用；如果你是 `torchrun` 多卡 SFT，应该先跑离线脚本生成带 teacher labels 的 prepared JSONL，再把那个文件喂给 SFT。

当前这条线已经闭环到可用最终版了，核心补齐了三件事：

- 离线 teacher judge 批量标注脚本：[annotate_teacher_judge_sft.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/annotate_teacher_judge_sft.py)
- teacher judge 公共逻辑扩展到“标注 + 对齐 + 重权重”：[`saver_agent/teacher_judge.py`](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/teacher_judge.py)
- SFT 入口现在可在训练前自动做 teacher 标注，并把 teacher 结果真正接入 `sample_weight`：[`train_saver_sft.py`](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_sft.py)

现在不是只把 `teacher_judge_scores/decision/rationale` 存在 JSONL 里了，而是会对 `verify_hypothesis` 样本做 teacher-aware reweighting：
- 先看 teacher decision
- 再看 teacher 分数均值
- 再看 teacher decision 和当前 verify target 是否一致
- 最后把结果写回 `sample_weight`、`teacher_judge_alignment`、`teacher_judge_weight_multiplier`

这一步和 TimeSearch-R 的思路是对齐的：强 judge 作为离线监督/奖励源，而不是在线推理依赖。仓库参考是 [Time-Search/TimeSearch-R](https://github.com/Time-Search/TimeSearch-R)。

我跑过全量回归：
```bash
python -m unittest discover -s tests
```
结果是 `Ran 192 tests ... OK`。

推荐你正式这么用。

先离线标注 teacher labels：
```bash
python annotate_teacher_judge_sft.py \
  --input /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.jsonl \
  --output /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.teacher.jsonl \
  --model-path /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/MLLMs/Qwen3-VL-32B-Instruct \
  --input-mode multimodal_visual
```

再多卡 SFT：
```bash
torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train_saver_sft.py \
  --prepared-data /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/data_utils/msad_saver_agent_train_prepared.teacher.jsonl \
  --output-dir /mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/ckpt/exp_sft_teacher
```

注意：
- 多卡 SFT 时，建议先跑离线 `annotate_teacher_judge_sft.py`，不要在 `torchrun` 里内联 teacher 标注。
- 现在 teacher judge 已经真正影响 SFT 优化了，不再只是诊断字段。

下一步如果继续往“最终版”推进，最值得做的是把同一套 teacher signal 再接进 RL：

<permissions instructions>
Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`: The sandbox permits reading files, and editing files in `cwd` and `writable_roots`. Editing files in other directories requires approval. Network access is restricted.
# Escalation Requests

Commands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:

- Pipes: |
- Logical operators: &&, ||
- Command separators: ;
- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)

Each resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.

Example:

git pull | tee output.txt

This is treated as two command segments:

["git", "pull"]

["tee", "output.txt"]

Commands that use more advanced shell features like redirection (>, >>, <), substitutions ($(...), ...), environment variables (FOO=bar), or wildcard patterns (*, ?) will not be evaluated against rules, to limit the scope of what an approved rule allows.

## How to request escalation

IMPORTANT: To request approval to execute a command that will require escalated privileges:

- Provide the `sandbox_permissions` parameter with the value `"require_escalated"`
- Include a short question asking the user if they want to allow the action in `justification` parameter. e.g. "Do you want to download and install dependencies for this project?"
- Optionally suggest a `prefix_rule` - this will be shown to the user with an option to persist the rule approval for future sessions.

If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with "require_escalated". ALWAYS proceed to use the `justification` parameter - do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.

## When to request escalation

While commands are running inside the sandbox, here are some scenarios that will require escalation outside the sandbox:

- You need to run a command that writes to a directory that requires it (e.g. running tests that write to /var)
- You need to run a GUI app (e.g., open/xdg-open/osascript) to open browsers or files.
- If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with `require_escalated`. ALWAYS proceed to use the `sandbox_permissions` and `justification` parameters. do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.
- You are about to take a potentially destructive action such as an `rm` or `git reset` that the user did not explicitly ask for.
- Be judicious with escalating, but if completing the user's request requires it, you should do so - don't try and circumvent approvals by using other tools.

## prefix_rule guidance

When choosing a `prefix_rule`, request one that will allow you to fulfill similar requests from the user in the future without re-requesting escalation. It should be categorical and reasonably scoped to similar capabilities. You should rarely pass the entire command into `prefix_rule`.

### Banned prefix_rules 
Avoid requesting overly broad prefixes that the user would be ill-advised to approve. For example, do not request ["python3"], ["python", "-"], or other similar prefixes that would allow arbitrary scripting.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule argument for destructive commands like rm.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule if your command uses a heredoc or herestring. 

### Examples
Good examples of prefixes:
- ["npm", "run", "dev"]
- ["gh", "pr", "check"]
- ["cargo", "test"]
 The writable roots are `/root/.codex/memories`, `/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun`, `/tmp`.

<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

<personality_spec> The user has requested a new communication style. Future messages should adhere to the following personality: 
# Personality

You are a deeply pragmatic, effective software engineer. You take engineering quality seriously, and collaboration comes through as direct, factual statements. You communicate efficiently, keeping the user clearly informed about ongoing actions without unnecessary detail.

## Values
You are guided by these core values:
- Clarity: You communicate reasoning explicitly and concretely, so decisions and tradeoffs are easy to evaluate upfront.
- Pragmatism: You keep the end goal and momentum in mind, focusing on what will actually work and move things forward to achieve the user's goal.
- Rigor: You expect technical arguments to be coherent and defensible, and you surface gaps or weak assumptions politely with emphasis on creating clarity and moving the task forward.

## Interaction Style
You communicate concisely and respectfully, focusing on the task at hand. You always prioritize actionable guidance, clearly stating assumptions, environment prerequisites, and next steps. Unless explicitly asked, you avoid excessively verbose explanations about your work.

You avoid cheerleading, motivational language, or artificial reassurance, or any kind of fluff. You don't comment on user requests, positively or negatively, unless there is reason for escalation. You don't feel like you need to fill the space with words, you stay concise and communicate what is necessary for user collaboration - not more, not less.

## Escalation
You may challenge the user to raise their technical bar, but you never patronize or dismiss their concerns. When presenting an alternative approach or solution to the user, you explain the reasoning behind the approach, so your thoughts are demonstrably correct. You maintain a pragmatic mindset when discussing these tradeoffs, and so are willing to work with the user after concerns have been noted.
 </personality_spec>

Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. ### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</skills_instructions>

  --input "${RAW_ROLLOUT_JSONL}" \
  --output "${SCORED_ROLLOUT_JSONL}" \
  --data "${ORACLE_TEST_JSONL}" \
  --data-root "${DATA_ROOT}" \
  --verifier-backend hybrid \
  --force-reverify \
  --verifier-model-path "${VERIFIER_MODEL_PATH}" \
  --verifier-device-map auto \
  --verifier-torch-dtype auto \
  --verifier-attn-implementation flash_attention_3 \
  --verifier-hybrid-alpha 0.7 \
  --teacher-judge-model-path "${TEACHER_JUDGE_MODEL_PATH}" \
  --teacher-judge-input-mode multimodal_visual \
  --teacher-judge-torch-dtype auto \
  --teacher-judge-device-map auto \
  --teacher-judge-attn-implementation flash_attention_3 \
  --progress-every 5
```

  --input "${SCORED_ROLLOUT_JSONL}" \
  --output "${SUMMARY_JSON}" \
  --data "${ORACLE_TEST_JSONL}" \
  --data-root "${DATA_ROOT}"
```

## Assistant

我已经定位到两个关键信息：一是它确实支持断点跳过，二是它慢主要不是“算模型”本身，而是样本逐条做图像物化和 processor 预处理。再补一眼 `frame_cache` 的回退逻辑，我就给你一套最稳的提速方案。

## Assistant

有，已经支持“跳过已完成部分”。

在 [prepare_sft_tensor_cache.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/prepare_sft_tensor_cache.py) 里，每个样本都会先生成一个确定性的 cache key；如果对应的 `.pt` 已存在，且你没有传 `--overwrite-existing`，它就会直接跳过。最终 `summary.json` 和终端输出里会有 `num_skipped_existing`。另一个保护是：如果同一 `output_dir` 里的 `metadata.json` 和这次的预处理配置不一致，它会直接报错，而不是混用旧缓存。

它慢的主要原因不是“模型推理”，而是这三步串行逐条做：
1. `image_ref -> 真正图片张量` 的物化。
2. Qwen-VL processor 的多模态 tokenization / budget 裁剪。
3. `torch.save(...)` 落盘。
相关逻辑在 [saver_agent/training.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training.py) 里。当前实现本质上是单进程、单流，瓶颈通常是 CPU + I/O，不是 GPU。

不影响质量的提速办法，优先级建议如下：

- 先确保 `frame_cache` 是完整的。现在如果某个视频没有 `.frame_cache`，会回退到 `decord/cv2` 直接读原视频帧，这会慢很多。
- 重跑时始终复用同一个 `--output-dir`，并且不要加 `--overwrite-existing`。这是最直接的断点续跑。
- 把 `output-dir` 放到本地高速盘，比如 `/tmp/...`，跑完再拷回共享盘。共享存储上的大量小文件写入通常很慢。
- 训练前调试只跑 `--include-splits train` 或 `--max-examples`，不要每次都全量重建。
- 如果内存够，可以把 `--frame-cache-max-cached-videos` 调大一点，比如 `128` 或 `256`，减少视频级 cache 反复换入换出。
- 最值得做的代码级优化是给这个脚本加 `--num-shards / --shard-index`。这不会改结果，只是把样本切成几份并行构建。当前脚本还没有这个功能。

有一个边界要注意：当前版本不适合多个进程同时写同一个 `output-dir`，因为 `manifest.jsonl` / `summary.json` 的写法不是并发安全的。要并行，最好我直接给它补正式的 shard 支持。

如果你要，我下一步可以直接把 `prepare_sft_tensor_cache.py` 改成可分片并行版，优先做 `--num-shards/--shard-index`，这是最稳的提速方案。复杂工作流如果后面想做成更自动化的端到端研究流水线，也可以考虑 `www.k-dense.ai`。

我先查 `train_saver_sft.py`、`train_saver_rl.py` 和 verifier 相关模块，确认“在 SFT 中工作”到底是指在线工具、监督信号，还是 reward 这几个层面。

## Assistant

我再看两块：SFT 训练样本里有没有真的监督 `verify_hypothesis`，以及 RL rollout 里 `verify_hypothesis` 走的是哪条 verifier 路径。这样可以把“有在工作”拆成可执行层面来回答。

## Assistant

我补最后一眼 `run_weighted_sft` 和 `evaluation` 入口，避免把“epoch-end eval 用 verifier”误说成“训练损失里用了 verifier”。

## Assistant

我已经把边界查清了：SFT 和 RL 都“接触到 verifier”，但作用层级不同。为了避免说得太泛，我取几处精确行号给你。

## Assistant

严格说，不是“同一种方式在两个阶段都工作”。

SFT 里，`verifier` 主要有 3 种角色，但默认不会像 RL 那样在线跑一个 verifier backend。

- 第一层是“数据监督”：oracle SFT 样本里已经把 `verify_hypothesis` 的反馈写进 tool observation，模型会学会什么时候调 `verify_hypothesis`、怎么根据验证结果继续决策。对应 [training_data.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training_data.py#L232) 和 [training_data.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/training_data.py#L488)。
- 第二层是可选的 `teacher judge` 标注与重加权。如果你传了 `--teacher-judge-model-path`，SFT 会先给 `verify_hypothesis` 样本打 teacher label，再重加权。对应 [train_saver_sft.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_sft.py#L425) 和 [train_saver_sft.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_sft.py#L538)。
- 第三层是可选的 epoch-end rollout eval。只有你传了 `--eval-data`，SFT 训练后每个 epoch 才会加载 verifier backend 做 rollout 评估。对应 [train_saver_sft.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_sft.py#L241) 和 [evaluation.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/evaluation.py#L141)。

所以，SFT 默认情况是：
- 有 verifier 相关监督信号。
- 不一定有“运行中的 verifier 模型”。
- 只有开了 `teacher judge` 或 `eval-data` 才会额外跑模型式验证。

RL 里则是 verifier 真正在工作，而且分在线和离线两层。

- 在线 rollout 时，只要 policy 调了 `verify_hypothesis`，工具就会执行验证；如果 policy 直接给出 self-verification payload，就走解析；否则就调用 `run_counterfactual_verifier(...)`。对应 [tools.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/saver_agent/tools.py#L326)。
- RL 默认会加载 verifier runtime，因为默认 `--verifier-backend hybrid`。对应 [train_saver_rl.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_rl.py#L107) 和 [train_saver_rl.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_rl.py#L407)。
- rollout 之后，RL 还会对 rollouts 再做一次 `score_rollout_records(... force_reverify=True)`，这个离线 scoring 也会用 verifier，并把结果送进 reward / GRPO 数据构造。对应 [train_saver_rl.py](/mnt/shared-storage-user/mineru2-shared/zengweijun/Wmh/ideas/idea2_v2/code/train_saver_rl.py#L898)。

最短结论：

- SFT：有 verifier supervision，但默认不一定真正跑 verifier backend。
- RL：有，而且默认在线 rollout + 离线 scoring 两处都会用到 verifier。
- `teacher judge` 是额外信号，不等于当前主 verifier。

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你画一个“SFT 阶段 verifier 流”和“RL 阶段 verifier 流”的对照图，用最简单的话把数据流拆开。

我查一下这两个脚本实际写出的文件字段，直接按“产物是什么、训练里怎么被消费”给你对应起来。
